sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: AMD Stock After Hours (Dec. 9, 2025): China AI Chip Breakthrough and 7 Things to Know Before Tomorrow’s Open
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (NASDAQ: AMD) saw its stock remain relatively flat on December 9, 2025, hovering around $221 per share, after news broke that the U.S. will allow Nvidia's H200 AI chips to be exported to China, with AMD's AI accelerators expected to receive similar treatment. Wall Street analysts are increasingly bullish on AMD's AI story, with "Buy" ratings and price targets between $240 and $280, driven by strategic AI partnerships and expected growth in AI inference workloads. Important upcoming catalysts include AMD CFO Jean Hu's presentation at Barclays' conference and the Federal Reserve's rate decision, which could inject volatility into the stock while investors balance growth potential with rich valuations and insider selling.

feed.title: China reportedly set to limit access to Nvidia’s H200 chips after Trump green lights export
feed.summary: Beijing is reportedly planning to limit access to Nvidia's advanced H200 AI chips, even after U.S. President Donald Trump indicated he would allow their export to China with a 25-percent fee. This move would create additional obstacles for Nvidia and other top U.S. chipmakers in the Chinese market, which has been subject to previous U.S. restrictions on advanced AI processors. The decision comes as Beijing continues to push back against the use of U.S. technology by its domestic firms.

feed.title: Beijing To Restrict Nvidia's H200 Chip Access Despite Trump's Approval: Report
feed.summary: Beijing reportedly plans to restrict access to Nvidia's advanced H200 chips, despite former President Trump's approval for their export to China. Chinese buyers will need to justify why they cannot meet their AI chip needs through domestic providers before purchasing, aiming to boost local chipmakers. This move follows increased customs checks and an acceleration of domestic chip production to reduce reliance on foreign AI chips.

feed.title: How Trump’s U-Turn on Nvidia Chips Changes the Game for China’s AI
feed.summary: President Trump has controversially allowed Nvidia to sell its H200 AI chips to China, a decision that critics argue will significantly aid Beijing's artificial intelligence development. This move benefits Nvidia by securing a large market but also provides China with advanced semiconductors it couldn't produce independently, potentially reshaping the U.S.-China technological competition.

feed.title: How Trump’s U-Turn on Nvidia Chips Changes the Game for China’s AI
feed.summary: President Trump has made a controversial decision by allowing Nvidia to sell its H200 artificial-intelligence chips to China, a move that critics fear will help Beijing catch up in the technological Cold War. This decision grants Nvidia access to a massive market while simultaneously providing China's AI industry with advanced semiconductors it couldn't produce domestically, potentially altering the balance of AI capabilities between the two nations.

feed.title: How Trump’s U-Turn on Nvidia Chips Changes the Game for China’s AI
feed.summary: President Trump has allowed Nvidia to sell its H200 artificial-intelligence chips to China, a move that critics fear will help Beijing's AI industry catch up to the U.S. This decision gives Nvidia access to a massive market while providing China with crucial high-end semiconductors it couldn't develop domestically. The deal significantly reshapes the technological competition between the two countries.

feed.title: How Trump’s U-Turn on Nvidia Chips Changes the Game for China’s AI
feed.summary: President Trump has approved the export of Nvidia's H200 AI chips to China, a move that has significant implications for both the U.S. and China's AI industries. While it provides Nvidia with a crucial market, critics fear it accelerates China's AI development by supplying technology it cannot produce domestically, reshaping the technological competition between the two nations. This decision emerged hours after the Justice Department announced a smuggling case targeting Beijing.

feed.title: How Trump’s U-Turn on Nvidia Chips Changes the Game for China’s AI

feed.title: SEALSQ boosts quantum investment fund to over $100 million By Investing.com

feed.title: Supermicro (SMCI) launches 4U NVIDIA HGX B300 with up to 98% heat capture
feed.summary: Supermicro (SMCI) has announced the commercial availability of new liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX B300 systems in 4U and 2-OU (OCP) form factors, designed for high-density hyperscale and AI factory deployments. These systems feature DLC-2 liquid cooling, capturing up to 98% of system heat for up to 40% power savings, and can support up to 144 GPUs per rack in the 2-OU OCP platform. feed.title: Exclusive-Nvidia builds location verification tech that could help fight chip smuggling

Initially available for new Blackwell chips, this feature could address U.S. calls to prevent illegal exports, though it has also raised concerns in China about potential backdoors.

feed.title: Intel, AMD Accused of Failing to Block Chips in Russian Missiles
feed.summary: Intel Corp., Advanced Micro Devices Inc., and Texas Instruments Inc. are facing multiple lawsuits, accusing them of failing to prevent their microchips from being used in Russian-made weapons. The lawsuits allege "willful ignorance" by the companies regarding third-party resale of restricted chips to Russia, in violation of US sanctions, for use in drones and missiles against Ukrainian civilians. One of the five suits specifically includes a company owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc.

feed.title: Intel, AMD Accused of Allowing Chips in Russian Missiles
feed.summary: Intel, AMD, and Texas Instruments are facing lawsuits alleging "willful ignorance" in allowing their microchips to be resold to Russia for use in weapons against Ukraine, violating US sanctions. These lawsuits, filed on behalf of Ukrainian civilians, cite multiple attacks between 2023 and 2025 involving drones and missiles containing components from these companies. The firms, along with Mouser Electronics (a Berkshire Hathaway company), are accused of domestic corporate negligence and failing to prevent the diversion of their technology.

feed.title: Qualcomm Acquires Ventana to Advance RISC-V CPUs and Cut Arm Reliance
feed.summary: Qualcomm has acquired Ventana Micro Systems to bolster its RISC-V processor development, aiming to enhance custom CPU designs like the Oryon line for data centers and edge devices. This strategic move is intended to reduce Qualcomm's reliance on Arm's instruction set, promote open architectures, and position the company as a leader in innovative, hybrid chip designs. The acquisition taps into the growing RISC-V market and signals a broader industry shift towards customizable and cost-efficient alternatives to proprietary technologies.

feed.title: Oracle stock price falls, taking Nvidia and other AI chip giants with it: Why tech are shares reeling today?
feed.summary: Oracle's stock price plummeted over 12% following its Q2 2026 earnings report, which, despite beating EPS expectations, missed revenue forecasts. This dip, alongside renewed concerns about "circular spending" in the AI industry, has caused a decline in the stock prices of major AI chip companies and other prominent tech firms, signaling potential fears of an AI bubble.

feed.title: HPE dukes it out with Cisco, Huawei as Juniper muscle kicks in
feed.summary: Following its acquisition of Juniper Networks, HPE is making significant strides in the router and switch markets, challenging established players like Cisco and Huawei. New data from Dell'Oro Group shows HPE's ethernet campus switch revenue growing above market rate, largely attributed to the Juniper acquisition and a strategic focus on AIOps. The company aims to consolidate its portfolio and leverage integrated AI and networking solutions to capture market share, with early indications of success despite the early stage of the integration.

feed.title: If Google wins AI race, Nvidia is 'in trouble,' says author of Jensen Huang biography
feed.summary: Stephen Witt, author of a Jensen Huang biography, warns that Nvidia's AI dominance could be threatened if Google's Gemini model proves successful with its homegrown Tensor Processing Units (TPUs). This competition, along with other rivals, poses a "huge risk" to Nvidia's stock. Witt also notes the absence of a clear succession plan for CEO Jensen Huang, suggesting Nvidia's high valuation heavily relies on Huang's vision and leadership.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Rises as Nvidia Wins China Chip Approval Under New U.S. Rule
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose after Nvidia received U.S. approval to sell its H200 AI chips to China under a new revenue-sharing model. This development has investors anticipating that a similar approval could extend to AMD's MI300 line, which has previously been restricted from the Chinese market. Such an authorization would significantly boost AMD's data center forecast and competitive position in a major AI chip market.

feed.title: AMD Stock: Latest Price, AI Growth Outlook and Wall Street Forecasts After the November 21 Sell-Off
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) is a closely watched AI and semiconductor stock. Following a sharp sell-off on November 21, 2025, AMD's stock has stabilized and analysts remain largely positive due to strong AI and data center growth prospects, despite some valuation concerns and intense competition. The company's long-term strategy focuses on a significant increase in revenue, especially from its data center AI business, with ambitious targets backed by new products, partnerships (like OpenAI), and a growing total addressable market.

feed.title: Adobe Stock on December 10, 2025: Q4 Earnings Preview, AI Strategy, Semrush Deal and Wall Street Forecasts
feed.summary: Adobe Inc. is reporting its Q4 2025 earnings with strong fundamentals and accelerating AI-related revenue, despite a lagging stock performance. Wall Street anticipates a ~$6.11 billion revenue and ~$5.40 non-GAAP EPS, with high expectations for an EPS beat. The company's AI strategy, including Firefly and Acrobat AI Assistant, the Semrush acquisition, and ChatGPT integration, are key factors influencing its future growth narrative and investor sentiment.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Names New CFO As Chip Demand Heats Up
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries has appointed Sam Franklin as its new Chief Financial Officer, signaling a strategic move as the company expands its manufacturing operations outside Asia. This appointment comes amidst surging demand for semiconductors, particularly those powering artificial intelligence, and positions GlobalFoundries for growth with key facilities in the US, Singapore, and Germany. The company also beat analyst revenue estimates for the fourth quarter, further highlighting its strong position in the evolving chipmaking industry.

feed.title: Arteris Fortifies AI-Driven Future with Strategic Acquisition of Cycuity, Championing Semiconductor Cybersecurity
feed.summary: Arteris Inc. (NASDAQ: APLS) announced its acquisition of Cycuity, Inc., a pioneer in semiconductor cybersecurity assurance, aiming to integrate robust hardware security verification into its system IP for chiplet and System-on-Chip (SoC) creation. This strategic move addresses the escalating cyber threats targeting silicon and emphasizes a "secure by design" approach for AI-driven systems. By combining Cycuity's Radix software with Arteris's Network-on-Chip interconnect IP, the acquisition facilitates early vulnerability detection in chip designs, strengthening the foundation for secure AI innovation.

feed.title: AMD (AMD) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of AMD fell 3.8% due to disappointing earnings from Oracle, raising concerns about enterprise AI spending, and a US policy shift benefiting Nvidia in the China market. This has led to fears that companies with tightening budgets may opt for Nvidia over AMD. Despite the drop, AMD is up 77.8% year-to-date, though it's still below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Nasdaq Trading Recap for December 11, 2025: Volume Leaders & Price Action - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Trading activity on the Nasdaq exchange on December 11, 2025, showed Agape ATP Corporation as the highest volume stock, with other significant gainers like VS Media Holdings Ltd. and Work Medical Technology Group also performing well. Major technology stocks like NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple experienced declines, while Microsoft was an exception, gaining on the day. Additionally, the report notes significant losses for Rezolute Inc., Robinhood Markets Inc., and Broadcom Inc., along with drops in the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF and iShares Ethereum Trust ETF.

feed.title: Intel, AMD And Texas Instruments Sued Over Chips Allegedly Ending Up In Russian Weapons
feed.summary: Intel, AMD, and Texas Instruments are facing multiple lawsuits in a Texas state court. Ukrainian plaintiffs allege the companies failed to prevent their chips from being diverted to Russia and integrated into weapons used against Ukrainian civilians. The lawsuits also implicate a Berkshire Hathaway-owned distributor, Mouser Electronics, for allegedly facilitating the routing of components through shell companies linked to Russian proxies.

feed.title: What’s Next For Air Products Stock?
feed.summary: Air Products & Chemicals (NYSE: APD) experienced a nearly 25% stock decline in 2025, largely due to a strategic reset involving the exit from several capital-heavy clean energy projects, resulting in a $2.3 billion charge. This, combined with underlying business challenges like weak volume trends and macroeconomic pressures, and leadership changes, created significant investor uncertainty. Despite these issues and a muted growth outlook, APD maintains healthy cash flows and a stable dividend, suggesting a potential recovery reliant on restored margin growth and a clearer, more cautious strategy.

feed.title: AMD Shares Rise 5% After Key Trading Signal
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) saw its shares rise by nearly 5% after triggering a "Power Inflow" signal on December 11 at 10:02 AM EST. This bullish indicator, based on order flow analytics, suggested a strong shift towards buying activity from both institutional and retail traders. The stock, which had initially dropped 2%, rebounded to an intraday high of $221.21 from $210.84, demonstrating the signal's effectiveness in identifying potential bullish momentum and short-term trading opportunities.

feed.title: AMD Stock After Hours on December 11, 2025: What Investors Need to Know Before the December 12 Open
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) experienced a highly volatile session on December 11, 2025, with a 5% intraday swing caused by Oracle's warnings on AI spending and uncertainty surrounding China export rules, yet ultimately closed flat. Despite these headwinds, the stock rebounded strongly from intraday lows due to a "Power Inflow" alert and remains a significant AI performer for the year. Investors are now watching for further developments on AI spending, China export policies, and technical levels before the market opens on December 12, 2025.

feed.title: Micron (MU) After Hours: What to Know Before the Stock Market Opens on December 12, 2025
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) closed Thursday down 2% but rebounded slightly after hours. The stock, which is up over 200% year-to-date, saw weakness due to Oracle's disappointing earnings, which affected other AI-linked tech stocks. Despite this, analysts have raised price targets for Micron, underscoring strong demand for its AI memory products and a strategic shift towards higher-margin data center business.

feed.title: Arm Holdings (ARM) Stock on December 11, 2025: Oracle Shock, 200‑Day Breakdown and the AI Growth Story
feed.summary: Arm Holdings (ARM) stock experienced a significant drop on December 11, 2025, falling below its 200-day moving average due to Oracle's disappointing earnings and broader AI sector skepticism. Despite robust Q2 fiscal 2026 results and strong AI growth forecasts from analysts, the stock's valuation remains a point of debate, with RISC-V competition, China market dynamics, and SoftBank's majority ownership identified as key risks. The article suggests that Arm is facing short-term pain driven by market sentiment, but its long-term leverage to AI infrastructure remains strong.

feed.title: Supermicro (NASDAQ: SMCI) distributor Azio AI backs Philippines sovereign AI infrastructure talks
feed.summary: Azio AI, a Supermicro distributor, is engaged in early-stage talks with the Philippines to support the modernization of its Maharlika Investment Fund and corporation. This involves evaluating GPU-based AI data centers and sovereign compute architectures as part of the country's 2024–2025 planning cycle. The initiative aligns with Azio AI's strategy to expand into sovereign and public-sector AI infrastructure, although any commercial engagement is subject to definitive agreements.

feed.title: Himax Technologies (NasdaqGS:HIMX): Reassessing Valuation After New WiseEye AI, Micro-Display and CPO Progress
feed.summary: Himax Technologies is gaining momentum with its WiseEye AI, FrontLED micro-display, and progress in Co-Packaged Optics for AI data centers, leading to a 26.74% year-to-date share price return. Despite trading above consensus price targets, its 26.7x P/E ratio is below the US semiconductor average of 37.9x, suggesting potential undervaluation. The company's unique technologies in ultra-low power sensing and microdisplay position it well for growth in smart glasses and AR markets, though near-term demand weakness and trade tensions pose risks.

feed.title: Intel Stock (INTC) Slides After the Bell on December 11, 2025: CEO Scrutiny, Lawsuits and EU Fine – What to Know Before the December 12 Open
feed.summary: Intel's stock (INTC) experienced a significant drop on December 11, 2025, due to scrutiny over CEO Lip-Bu Tan's dealmaking, lawsuits alleging Intel chips in Russian weaponry, and an EU antitrust fine. Despite being a turnaround story in 2025, the stock faces challenges including governance issues, reputational risks, and operational hurdles in its AI strategy. Investors are watching for company responses to these issues and overall semiconductor market trends before the December 12 open, with analysts holding a cautious outlook.

feed.title: Why Oracle Sold Its Stake In Chip Designer Ampere, According to Larry Ellison
feed.summary: Oracle Chairman Larry Ellison stated the company sold its stake in chip designer Ampere Computing because it is no longer strategic to design, manufacture, and use its own chips in cloud data centers. This decision aligns with Oracle's new policy of "chip neutrality," enabling them to work with various CPU and GPU suppliers and deploy chips based on customer demand. The sale resulted in a $2.7 billion pre-tax gain for Oracle, with SoftBank Group acquiring Ampere for $6.5 billion to bolster its AI capabilities.

feed.title: Arteris to acquire cybersecurity firm Cycuity to enhance chip security By Investing.com
feed.summary: Arteris, Inc. (NASDAQ:AIP) announced its definitive agreement to acquire semiconductor cybersecurity firm Cycuity, Inc., aiming to bolster chip security solutions for designers. The acquisition addresses the rising threat of cyberattacks on semiconductors, with hardware vulnerabilities increasing significantly in recent years. This strategic move, supported by Arteris' strong financial position, is expected to close in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026 and will enhance Arteris' system IP portfolio with Cycuity's capabilities in identifying security weaknesses across chip components.

feed.title: Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (NASDAQ:AMUU) Raises Dividend to $6.70 Per Share
feed.summary: Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (AMUU) announced a significant increase in its quarterly dividend to $6.7021 per share, representing a 2,716.1% increase from its previous payout, with a record date of December 10th and payable on December 17th. This dividend hike implies a 44.9% yield. Despite the substantial dividend increase, the stock experienced a 10.1% decline in trading on Wednesday, closing at $59.77.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices' Latest Meltdown Heightens Speculative Fervor For Direxion's AMUU, AMDD ETFs
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is experiencing high volatility, leading to increased speculative interest in Direxion's leveraged and inverse ETFs, AMUU and AMDD. While some analysts are optimistic about AMD's AI market share and valuation, recent significant declines in its stock price and large sell-offs by Ark Invest have raised concerns. The article details how these ETFs allow traders to speculate on AMD's price movements, emphasizing the risks associated with such leveraged instruments.

feed.title: Texas Instruments, Intel Corporation microchips in Russian missiles, lawsuits say
feed.summary: Several lawsuits accuse Texas Instruments, Intel Corporation, Advanced Micro Devices, and Mouser Electronics of supplying microchips found in Russian missiles used in attacks against Ukrainian civilians. The lawsuits allege these companies knowingly or unknowingly contributed to wrongful deaths by allowing their products to be used in Russian and Iranian military weapons, despite U.S. export laws. The accused companies generally deny direct involvement with the Russian military, stating their compliance with export laws and cessation of business in Russia.

feed.title: AI Boom: Bubble Signs, But Opportunities Remain
feed.summary: David Eiswert of T. Rowe Price explores the characteristics of an AI investment bubble, noting rapid capital allocation and market frenzy, but emphasizes that the sector remains in undersupply. He argues that AI is currently generating significant economic surplus and offers a competitive advantage, with manageable risks. The primary concern for a bursting bubble, an oversupply of AI capacity, has not yet materialized; instead, there is a clear undersupply.

feed.title: ‘China’s little Nvidia’ warns of overheating risks after 723% share surge
feed.summary: Moore Threads Technology, a Chinese GPU maker dubbed "China's little Nvidia," has issued a warning to investors about overheating risks after its shares surged 723% since its debut. The company stated there was no new catalyst or change in fundamentals, prompting concerns about irrational speculation. Shares dropped 13% after the warning, reflecting the company's caution to investors to make prudent decisions amidst the rapid rise.

feed.title: AI Chips Update - Navitas Expands Global Reach With Avnet Partnership
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor has expanded its global distribution network through a partnership with Avnet, aiming to provide consistent worldwide access and technical support for its GaN and SiC high-voltage semiconductor devices. This collaboration focuses on high-growth markets like AI data centers, high-performance computing, and industrial electrification, leveraging Avnet's global reach. The article also touches on other market news including Winbond Electronics and Moore Threads Technology, and highlights NVIDIA's investment potential in AI.

feed.title: Nvidia's future-forward take on the AI semiconductor boom
feed.summary: Jensen Huang, CEO of Nvidia, believes AI data centers will become "intelligence factories" generating real-time intelligence, ushering in a new computing era. This shift presents the largest semiconductor opportunity in history as computing is reinvented from the ground up, with an estimated trillion-dollar annual investment in AI-driven data centers by 2030. Nvidia is positioning itself as the key architect of this transformation.

feed.title: Bayesian Capital Management LP Increases Stock Position in Microchip Technology Incorporated $MCHP
feed.summary: Bayesian Capital Management LP significantly increased its stake in Microchip Technology Inc. (MCHP) by 338.4% in Q2, acquiring an additional 58,200 shares to hold a total of 75,400 shares valued at $5.31 million. Microchip Technology currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $74.95, despite recent price objective adjustments from some firms. The company reported slightly better-than-expected quarterly earnings and offers a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: AI: Potentially the biggest productivity enhancer since electricity?
feed.summary: Artificial intelligence is poised to drive the most significant productivity surge since electrification, surpassing the impact of the internet. Despite speculative risks, current AI valuations are more rational than during the dot-com era, fueled by strong earnings growth from large-cap technology companies. Investors are advised to navigate this multi-year cycle by focusing on cash-rich innovators with "linchpin" technologies, improving fundamentals, and reasonable valuations, while also considering other investable trends like cybersecurity and e-commerce.

feed.title: Brazil Potash Trials AI Ore-Sorting Technology to Cut Costs and Boost Sustainability
feed.summary: Brazil Potash Corp. has initiated an AI-powered optical ore-sorting trial at its Autazes Project to improve potash extraction efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance sustainability. The technology, utilizing X-ray Transmission (XRT) and machine learning, aims to concentrate ore by approximately 50% underground, leading to lower energy consumption, reduced environmental impact, and significant cost savings. This innovative approach could redefine mining practices globally, particularly for critical minerals, by increasing resource efficiency and promoting sustainable operations.

feed.title: Intel loses EU antitrust appeal but dodges a bigger fine
feed.summary: Intel has lost its EU antitrust appeal but the fine was significantly reduced from €376 million to €237,105,540. The General Court upheld the ruling that Intel engaged in anticompetitive conduct through "naked restrictions" by making payments to PC makers for delaying or cancelling products using AMD processors. This legal saga has been ongoing since 2009 and may continue with further appeals.

feed.title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): Navigating the AI Frontier with Cloud Dominance
feed.summary: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) continues to demonstrate robust financial performance and technological leadership, primarily driven by its dominant position in cloud computing and aggressive investments in Artificial Intelligence (AI). Under CEO Satya Nadella, Microsoft has strategically shifted to a "cloud-first, mobile-first" approach, rapidly expanding its Azure cloud platform and deeply integrating AI across its products, leading to significant revenue growth and a strong market position. The company faces increased regulatory scrutiny and high AI-related capital expenditures, but its strategic pivots and strong financial health suggest continued long-term growth.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM/NYSE): Powering the Future of AI and Global Technology
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), the world's largest independent semiconductor foundry, is critically positioned at the forefront of the surging AI and high-performance computing (HPC) revolution. The company exhibits robust financial growth, unmatched technological leadership in advanced node manufacturing (3nm, 5nm, 2nm, and upcoming A16), and significant global expansion efforts to diversify its supply chain. Despite geopolitical risks and increasing competition, TSMC's strategic investments in R&D and advanced packaging are solidifying its dominant market share and ensuring its indispensable role in future technology.

feed.title: Barclays Sticks to Its Hold Rating for SanDisk Corp (SNDK)
feed.summary: Barclays analyst Thomas O’Malley maintained a Hold rating on SanDisk Corp (SNDK) with a price target of $220.00, noting O'Malley's strong track record in the Technology sector. This follows a similar Hold rating from J.P. Morgan, though Bernstein issued a Buy rating. Corporate insider sentiment for SNDK is currently negative, with increased insider selling reported.

feed.title: AMD Stock News Today (Dec. 12, 2025): AI “Bubble” Jitters Hit Chipmakers as Analysts Keep a Bullish Tilt on Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD)
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) faces AI "bubble" jitters as investors reassess growth following Broadcom's margin warning and Oracle's spending. Despite short-term volatility and market sentiment swings, analysts maintain a bullish outlook due to AMD's strong Q3 2025 results, its significant multi-year partnership with OpenAI, and ambitious data-center revenue targets. The market now demands clearer evidence that AI revenue translates into durable margins and cash generation.

feed.title: Nvidia employee says Microsoft's cooling system for Blackwell GPUs 'wasteful', company responds
feed.summary: An anonymous Nvidia employee has called Microsoft's cooling system for its new Blackwell GPUs in data centers "wasteful," despite Microsoft using liquid cooling. The concern highlights the significant energy consumption of a building-wide air cooling system that works in conjunction with the liquid cooling. Microsoft has responded by stating its commitment to carbon neutrality and developing zero-water cooling designs for future data centers.

feed.title: KLA Corporation: The Unseen Architect Powering the AI Revolution from Silicon to Superintelligence
feed.summary: KLA Corporation (KLAC) is highlighted as a critical enabler of the AI revolution, providing advanced process control and yield management solutions essential for manufacturing next-generation AI chips. The company's technology, including defect inspection and metrology tools, ensures the precision and quality of semiconductors, supporting major chip developers and foundries. Despite challenges like U.S. export controls and industry cyclicality, KLA is strategically positioned for continued growth, particularly in advanced packaging and AI-driven solutions.

feed.title: Cohu, Inc. Navigates Semiconductor Downturn with Strategic Focus on AI and Advanced Chip Quality Assurance
feed.summary: Cohu, Inc. (NASDAQ: COHU) is showing resilience in the challenging semiconductor industry by focusing on AI and High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) testing. The company's strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of Tignis and the launch of new products like the Krypton inspection platform and Neon HBM inspection systems, are crucial for ensuring the quality and performance of next-generation semiconductors. Cohu's integrated approach to thermal management and data analytics, exemplified by the Eclipse platform and DI-Core suite, positions it as a vital enabler for the AI revolution, providing reliable hardware foundations for advanced AI applications.

feed.title: Broadcom tumbles 11% despite blockbuster earnings as 'AI angst' weighs on Oracle, Nvidia
feed.summary: Broadcom's shares fell 11% despite exceeding Wall Street estimates, as investors pulled back from AI-related stocks. This "AI angst" also impacted Oracle and Nvidia, even as Broadcom reported strong AI chip sales growth and a significant backlog of orders, though with potential short-term margin pressures. Analysts largely remain bullish on Broadcom's long-term AI prospects.

feed.title: Barclays Sticks to Its Hold Rating for SanDisk Corp (SNDK)
feed.summary: Barclays maintained a Hold rating on SanDisk Corp (SNDK) with a price target of $220.00, according to an analyst report. SanDisk reported quarterly revenue of $2.31 billion and net profit of $112 million for the quarter ending October 3. Corporate insider sentiment towards the stock is negative, with an increase in insider selling over the past quarter.

feed.title: China Is ‘Rejecting’ Nvidia’s H200 Chips, Outfoxing US Strategy, Sacks Says
feed.summary: According to White House AI czar David Sacks, China is "rejecting" Nvidia's H200 AI chips, opting instead for domestically developed semiconductors. This move potentially outfoxes the US strategy, which aimed to allow sales of H200 chips to China to foster American competition against Chinese tech firms like Huawei. Sacks expressed uncertainty about the effectiveness of the current US approach.

feed.title: AMD Stock After Hours (Dec. 12, 2025): Why Shares Fell, What’s Happening After the Bell, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: AMD stock experienced a significant decline on Friday, December 12, 2025, driven by broader semiconductor and AI sector sentiment rather than AMD-specific news. Concerns about AI profitability and geopolitical factors impacting chip exports influenced investor worries. Despite the day's drop, analyst forecasts remain largely positive, with varying price targets for the stock.

feed.title: Intel Stock (INTC) After the Bell on Dec. 12, 2025: After-Hours Price, Key News Catalysts, Analyst Forecasts, and What to Know Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Intel stock (NASDAQ: INTC) closed down 4.30% to $37.81 on December 12, 2025, and saw minimal movement in after-hours trading, reflecting investor digestion of a headline-heavy week. A Reuters report on Intel testing chipmaking tools from a China-linked supplier, along with broader AI sector concerns, governance scrutiny, and regulatory pressure in Europe, contributed to the stock's "headline fragile" state. Investors are now looking to the next market open on Monday, December 15, to assess ongoing policy risks, semiconductor sentiment, and upcoming catalysts like CES 2026 and Q4 earnings expectations.

feed.title: Analysts’ Opinions Are Mixed on These Technology Stocks: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Adobe (ADBE) and Western Digital (WDC)
feed.summary: Analysts have issued mixed opinions on several technology stocks. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) received a Hold rating from Citi and Bernstein, with a consensus of Strong Buy. Adobe (ADBE) also got a Hold from Morgan Stanley and Citi, while the overall consensus is Moderate Buy. Western Digital (WDC) was rated Buy by Morgan Stanley and Mizuho Securities, aligning with a Strong Buy consensus.

feed.title: From hype to hard returns: AI enters a new phase
feed.summary: The AI landscape is evolving from merely potential to focusing on profitability, driving aggressive investment and real-world gains. Increased reliance on debt funding for substantial AI capital expenditures (capex) is putting pressure on firms to establish clear monetization strategies and introduces new risks. Despite these challenges, the long-term growth prospects for the AI sector remain compelling, particularly for hardware suppliers and hyperscalers, but investors must prioritize execution and financial resilience.

feed.title: Navitas semiconductor director sells $1.18 million in NVTS stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: A director at Navitas Semiconductor Corp (NASDAQ:NVTS), Gary Kent Wunderlich JR, sold 128,300 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $1.18 million. These sales occurred on December 10 and 11, 2025, after Navitas shares had seen a 150% return over the past year. Navitas has also been active with new SiC power semiconductor samples, expanded distribution with Avnet, and strategic partnerships with Cyient and GlobalFoundries.

feed.title: Direxion's MUU, MUD ETFs Allow Traders To Speculate On Micron's Semiconductor Crossroads
feed.summary: Direxion offers MUU and MUD ETFs for traders to speculate on Micron Technology's stock (MU), given the company's strong performance driven by AI demand for its semiconductors. While MU has seen significant gains, concerns about market cyclicality and valuation persist. MUU tracks 200% of MU's daily performance, and MUD tracks 100% of the inverse daily performance, providing leveraged or inverse exposure without engaging the options market, though these ETFs carry their own risks and are designed for short-term exposure.

feed.title: Gilder Gagnon Howe & Co. LLC Acquires 28,825 Shares of SiTime Corporation $SITM
feed.summary: Gilder Gagnon Howe & Co. LLC increased its stake in SiTime Corporation (NASDAQ:SITM) by 7.9% in the second quarter, now owning 394,085 shares valued at $83,972,000. Other institutional investors, including Alliancebernstein L.P., American Century Companies Inc., and Ameriprise Financial Inc., also made significant changes to their holdings. Insider transactions show recent sales by Fariborz Assaderaghi and Samsheer Ahamad, while several analysts have issued new price targets and ratings for the stock.

feed.title: Constellation Energy (CEG) Stock: Calpine Deal Milestones, Nuclear Restart Momentum, and What to Watch Next Week (Updated Dec. 13, 2025)
feed.summary: Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) experienced a volatile week ending December 12, 2025, with its stock closing down 7% on Friday, despite a modest weekly decline. The company is navigating significant events including progress on its $16.4 billion Calpine acquisition with DOJ agreement and FERC approval, and continued momentum in the nuclear energy sector bolstered by awards and federal support. Upcoming catalysts include a FERC meeting on data center co-location in PJM and further updates on Calpine deal mechanics, all contributing to a strong narrative but volatile trading environment for CEG.

feed.title: VGT vs. SOXX: Should Investors Choose a Broad Tech ETF or a Niche Semiconductor Fund?
feed.summary: This article compares two tech ETFs: the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), which focuses on semiconductors, and the Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT), which offers broader tech exposure. While VGT has a much lower expense ratio and more diversification, SOXX has delivered stronger returns in recent years due to its concentrated focus on the semiconductor industry. The choice between the two depends on an investor's risk tolerance and desired exposure to the tech sector.

feed.title: Dr. Nissan Cohen Joins Nano Dimension’s Board of Directors
feed.summary: Nano Dimension Ltd., a prominent supplier of Additively Manufactured Electronics and 3D printers, announced the appointment of Dr. Nissan Cohen to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Dr. Cohen, with a nearly 40-year career in scientific research, technology development, and executive management, replaces Mr. Yaron Eitan. Yoav Stern, Nano Dimension’s Chairman and CEO, highlighted Dr. Cohen's semiconductor industry background and experience in scaling companies as highly valuable for Nano Dimension's growth.

feed.title: Texas Instruments accused of enabling Ukrainian bombing deaths
feed.summary: A lawsuit has been filed against Texas Instruments, AMD, and Intel, alleging their microchips were used in foreign weapons responsible for the bombing deaths of Ukrainian civilians. The plaintiffs claim the companies failed to prevent the diversion of their technology through known supply chain gaps, leading to accusations of negligence, export-law violations, and wrongful death. The lawsuit highlights a pattern of alleged misconduct where the companies continued sales into high-risk regions despite warnings and public links to sanctioned entities.

feed.title: Microsoft's AI boss calls Elon Musk a 'bulldozer' with 'superhuman capabilities'
feed.summary: Mustafa Suleyman, CEO of Microsoft AI, described Elon Musk as a "bulldozer" with "superhuman capabilities to bend reality to his will" due to his impressive track record in disruptive industries like auto and space. Suleyman also praised Sam Altman as "courageous" for aggressively building AI data centers, and Demis Hassabis as a "great scientist" and polymath, highlighting his significant contributions to the field. Despite being competitors, Suleyman confirmed he maintains good relationships with his peers in AI.

feed.title: First Trust Dorsey Wright Momentum & Low Volatility ETF宣布季度股息｜CMoney 股市爆料同學會
feed.summary: First Trust Dorsey Wright Momentum & Low Volatility ETF (DVOL) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.1008 per share. The dividend will be paid on December 31, with the record and ex-dividend dates both on December 12. Investors are advised to monitor the ETF's dividend growth and strategies.

feed.title: First Watch Restaurant Group, Inc. $FWRG Holdings Lessened by Advent International L.P.
feed.summary: Advent International L.P. has reduced its stake in First Watch Restaurant Group, Inc. ($FWRG) by 23.4%, selling 4.5 million shares, but still holds 14.69 million shares valued at approximately $235.6 million, making it their fifth-largest holding. Despite missing EPS estimates last quarter, First Watch reported strong revenue of $316.02 million. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating from Wall Street analysts with an average price target of $21.09.

feed.title: Entero's Grid AI Prepares for Major U.S. Expansion with New Leadership Appointments to Accelerate AI Infrastructure Strategy
feed.summary: Entero's wholly owned subsidiary, Grid AI, is establishing a U.S.-based leadership team to accelerate its expansion into the U.S. market for AI-ready digital infrastructure. This move follows a significant surge in AI data center expansion and aims to position Grid AI to meet the growing demands of the global compute industry. The new executive team will bring deep experience in energy optimization and public markets governance to support hyperscale AI infrastructure development.

feed.title: AMD: High Growth At A Reasonable Price As AI Adoption Accelerates
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is a market leader in AI-driven high-performance computing, benefiting from strong demand in data centers, AI, and gaming. The company is considered attractively valued at a 34x FY2026 earnings multiple, given its premium margins and robust growth momentum. With a "Strong Buy" rating and a $258 price target, the article suggests a 17% upside, supported by AMD's low leverage and dominant semiconductor market positioning in the face of ongoing AI and HPC tailwinds.

feed.title: Nvidia Vs. Advanced Micro Devices: Determining The True AI Alpha-OLS Model (NASDAQ:NVDA)
feed.summary: This article evaluates Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) in the AI semiconductor space, concluding that NVDA is the premier choice due to superior risk-adjusted returns, high R-squared, and lower sensitivity to macroeconomic factors. AMD, while offering high convexity and asymmetric upside at a discount, is better suited for risk-tolerant investors seeking a value-rerating opportunity. The author recommends NVDA for core AI exposure and AMD as a high-volatility complement for aggressive portfolios.

feed.title: New quantum-classical AI model boosts accuracy in image recognition
feed.summary: WiMi Hologram Cloud Inc. (NASDAQ: WIMI) has launched a new hybrid quantum neural network (H-QNN) designed to enhance image multi-classification accuracy and computational efficiency. This H-QNN integrates classical convolutional neural networks for feature extraction with quantum neural networks for high-dimensional nonlinear mapping, using a three-stage architecture. The technology boasts superior generalization ability and stability compared to similar algorithms, leveraging techniques like PCA with angle encoding, parameter sharing, and an FPGA-accelerated quantum simulation module for nanosecond-level state updates.

Despite a significant stock decline, the firm views WSC as a compounding industrial services franchise with a dominant market position, although its prediction of a cyclical trough for modular unit utilization was premature. The fund's annualized return since inception is 19.5%, outperforming the S&P 500 by 5.3%.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Avnet (AVT) Could Change Following Expanded Navitas GaN/SiC Distribution Deal - Learn Why
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor expanded its global distribution agreement with Avnet, deepening their collaboration on GaN and SiC high-power semiconductor devices. This move aims to strengthen Avnet's position in high-growth power and computing markets and provide Navitas with a unified route to customers, potentially reshaping Avnet's investment narrative by balancing capital returns with investments in higher complexity segments. Avnet's narrative projects $25.5 billion revenue and $680.5 million earnings by 2028, leading to a fair value of $53.00, a 7% upside from its current price, though individual fair value estimates for Avnet vary significantly.

feed.title: Nvidia Is Not A Bubble, Here's Why (NASDAQ:NVDA)
feed.summary: Nvidia Corporation is not experiencing an "AI bubble," with forward P/E multiples that are reasonable compared to historical tech bubbles and current sector peers, unlike the dot-com era. The company's future growth is underpinned by the expansion of open-source AI, strategic acquisitions, a strong product roadmap including Rubin in 2026, and potential reopening of the Chinese market. These factors suggest continued strong returns and position NVDA as a strong buy.

feed.title: Quantum Enablers Poised for 40% Plus Growth in 2026: INTC, AVGO & TER
feed.summary: While the pure quantum hardware sector (e.g., IonQ, D-Wave) offers long-term potential, the more immediate investment opportunity lies in quantum enablers that provide the necessary infrastructure, semiconductors, and security frameworks. This article highlights Intel (INTC), Broadcom (AVGO), and Teradyne (TER) as key quantum enablers projected to achieve over 40% earnings growth in 2026. These companies benefit from real revenues by supporting quantum adoption through existing technologies, post-quantum cryptography, and advanced testing solutions, rather than solely relying on future quantum computer development.

feed.title: Forget NVDA: 2 AI Stocks Crushing it in 2025 With More Upside Ahead
feed.summary: While NVIDIA (NVDA) has seen significant gains due to the AI boom, two other AI stocks, Micron Technology (MU) and BigBear.ai Holdings (BBAI), have outperformed it this year and offer further upside. Micron is bullish on strong demand for its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, while BigBear.ai's acquisition of Ask Sage and its focus on secure AI deployment in defense sectors are expected to drive significant revenue growth. Both companies show strong projected earnings growth, with Micron holding a Zacks Rank #1 and BigBear.ai a Zacks Rank #2.

feed.title: Quantum Enablers Poised for 40% Plus Growth in 2026: INTC, AVGO & TER
feed.summary: This article highlights "quantum enablers" like Intel (INTC), Broadcom (AVGO), and Teradyne (TER) as compelling investment opportunities within the quantum computing space for 2026. The article projects over 40% earnings growth for these companies in 2026 due to their foundational roles in the emerging quantum ecosystem and their focus on real-world applications such as post-quantum cryptography.

feed.title: How a Silicon Valley dealmaker charmed Trump, giving Intel a lifeline
authors: Max A. Cherney and Jeffrey Dastin
feed.summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, initially targeted by Donald Trump for his China investments, successfully secured a deal for the U.S. government to invest billions for a nearly 10% stake in Intel. This crucial agreement provided a "lifeline" to the embattled chipmaker, bolstering its financial position and opening doors for new partnerships, despite ongoing questions about Tan's technical expertise for leading Intel. The deal highlights a potential shift towards the government taking equity stakes in strategic industries.

feed.title: AMD Challenges NVIDIA Blackwell Dominance with New Instinct MI350 Series AI Accelerators
feed.summary: AMD has launched its Instinct MI350 series of AI accelerators, including the flagship MI355X, which aims to challenge NVIDIA's dominance, particularly in AI inference tasks. The new series features the CDNA 4 architecture built on TSMC's 3nm process, offering superior memory capacity and cost efficiency for running large AI models. This move signals a significant shift in the AI hardware market, with major hyperscalers like Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle already adopting or engaging with AMD's new offerings.

feed.title: The AI PC Revolution: Intel, AMD, and Qualcomm Battle for NPU Performance Leadership in 2025
feed.summary: The article discusses the "AI PC" revolution in 2025, highlighting the fierce competition between Qualcomm, AMD, and Intel for NPU performance leadership. Qualcomm's Snapdragon X2 Elite excels in raw AI processing with 80 TOPS, AMD's Ryzen AI Max focuses on unified memory to run large language models locally, and Intel's Panther Lake emphasizes "Platform TOPS" combining CPU, NPU, and GPU power. This shift is reshaping the computing landscape, moving AI tasks to local devices, disrupting traditional SaaS models, and impacting the discrete GPU market.

feed.title: Samsung To Unveil First In-House GPU For Exynos 2600
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics is preparing to launch its first in-house mobile GPU, integrated into the Exynos 2600 processor for the upcoming Galaxy S26 series, aiming for greater control over smartphone components and reduced reliance on external chip architectures. While the GPU's underlying architecture is still licensed from AMD, Samsung plans to fully internalize both design and architecture by 2027. This move, along with advancements in AMD's AI chips and Samsung's memory chip approvals for Nvidia, highlights significant shifts in the competitive semiconductor landscape.

feed.title: QuickLogic Announces Expanded Scope of Strategic Radiation Hardened FPGA Contract
feed.summary: QuickLogic Corporation has announced an expanded scope for its Strategic Radiation Hardened (SRH) FPGA contract with the U.S. government, increasing its total ceiling value to $88 million. The project, sponsored by the DoD’s T&AM Program, now includes the GlobalFoundries 12LP fabrication process. QuickLogic has also completed the design and tape-out for an FPGA test chip to be fabricated using this process.

feed.title: AI: Potentially the biggest productivity enhancer since electricity?
feed.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) has the potential to drive the most significant productivity surge since the electrification era, surpassing even the internet's impact. Despite speculative risks, current AI valuations appear more rational compared to the dot-com era, largely due to strong earnings growth from major tech companies. Investors are advised to navigate this multiyear cycle by focusing on companies with "linchpin" technologies, strong fundamentals, and reasonable valuations, while leveraging asset class volatility.

feed.title: AI Boom: Bubble Signs, But Opportunities Remain
feed.summary: David Eiswert discusses the current state of the artificial intelligence (AI) sector, noting signs of a potential bubble due to rapid capital allocation and market frenzy. However, he emphasizes that the AI sector is still in undersupply, creating significant economic surplus and competitive advantages, especially for firms like T. Rowe Price. Eiswert concludes that while bubble characteristics are present, the pervasive undersupply and ongoing improvements mean major risks are still manageable, representing a continued opportunity for investors.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) up More Than 76% YTD, Is it Still a Buy?

feed.title: Oracle stock holds near $200 as year-end Fed minutes loom and AI spending worries linger
feed.summary: Oracle's stock is holding near $200 as investors focus on the company's increased spending plans for AI cloud capacity and how quickly these outlays will translate into profit. The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting minutes are a key near-term event for rate-sensitive tech shares, influencing sentiment around Oracle's AI infrastructure bets. The company's recent forecast of sales and profit below analyst estimates, coupled with sharply rising capital expenditures for AI, has intensified concerns about AI-linked outlays outpacing profits.

feed.title: Nvidia insists it isn’t Enron, but its AI deals are testing investor faith
feed.summary: Nvidia's extensive partnerships in the AI sector, including vendor financing-like arrangements with companies like OpenAI and CoreWeave, are raising concerns among investors despite the company's surging stock price. While Nvidia vehemently denies comparisons to past corporate scandals like Enron or Lucent, critics argue that the circular nature of these deals and reliance on special-purpose vehicles could expose the company to significant risk if AI growth slows, potentially leading to write-downs and impacting its financial health. The company maintains that its transparent dealings and the vast potential of the AI market will ensure its continued success.

feed.title: Nvidia stock closes higher on Groq licensing deal as Wall Street heads into year-end

This move strengthens Nvidia's position in the AI "inference" segment amid increasing competition. The deal occurred during thin, post-holiday trading, with investors also eyeing the "Santa Claus rally" and upcoming U.S. housing data and Fed minutes.

feed.title: AMD's Lisa Su Pays Tribute To Former IBM CEO Lou Gerstner: An 'Amazingly Curious' Leader Who Shaped Her Early Career
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su honored former IBM CEO Lou Gerstner, who passed away at 83, recognizing his significant influence on her early career. Su praised Gerstner as an "amazingly curious and insightful" leader in technology, acknowledging his pivotal role in IBM's turnaround. Gerstner's legacy continues to inspire technology executives, including Su, who orchestrated a similar revival at AMD.

feed.title: Nvidia spends $5B on Intel bailout, instantly gets $2.5B richer
feed.summary: Nvidia's $5 billion investment in Intel stock, finalized after FTC approval, has swiftly turned into a $2.5 billion profit for Nvidia, as Intel's share price surged past the acquisition cost. This strategic financial move is tied to a broader collaboration between the two tech giants to jointly develop next-generation chips for data centers and PCs, integrating Nvidia's NVLink technology and custom x86 CPUs. The deal showcases a significant partnership following Nvidia's failed attempt to acquire Arm, highlighting a shift towards collaboration rather than acquisition for market expansion.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer announces retirement of SVP of operations and transition plan
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. announced the retirement of George Kao, SVP of Operations, effective December 31, 2025. Tom Xiao, Senior Corporate Vice President of Engineering, will assume his responsibilities, and Kao will remain as a consultant to ensure a smooth transition. The company anticipates no operational disruptions from this leadership change.

feed.title: Intel stock rises after Nvidia closes $5 billion share purchase — what’s next for INTC
feed.summary: Intel's stock rose after Nvidia completed a $5 billion equity purchase, acquiring 214.8 million newly issued Intel shares at $23.28 each. Investors are now watching for updates on the Intel-Nvidia product roadmap and Intel's upcoming earnings report for insights into cost control, factory returns, and revenue from the partnership.

feed.title: Nvidia finalizes $5B purchase of Intel shares
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. has finalized its $5 billion purchase of Intel Corp. shares through a private placement, acquiring a 4.4% stake in a deal originally announced in September. This investment is part of a broader partnership focusing on engineering initiatives, including Intel developing CPUs optimized for Nvidia's GPUs using NVLink technology, which promises significantly faster data transfer. The collaboration also extends to consumer markets with Intel launching systems-on-chips featuring chiplets derived from Nvidia's desktop CPUs.

feed.title: TSMC officially begins 2nm chip volume production in Q4 2025
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has officially started volume production of its 2-nanometer chips in the fourth quarter of 2025, confirming its original roadmap for the next-generation technology. Production is currently centered at Fab 22 in Kaohsiung and utilizes the company's first-generation nanosheet transistor technology, offering significant improvements in performance, power consumption, and transistor density compared to previous generations. TSMC is also developing an enhanced N2P manufacturing process slated for mass production in the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.: How AMD Turned a Chip Portfolio into a Platform Strategy
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. has evolved from a challenger CPU vendor into a full-stack compute platform player, reshaping PCs, gaming consoles, and data centers with aggressive innovation. The company has shifted from selling individual chips to offering a coherent compute platform across CPUs, GPUs, console SoCs, and AI accelerators. This transformation is driven by a focus on performance-per-watt, chiplet design, broad market reach, and an open ecosystem, making AMD a central player in the evolving compute infrastructure.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.: How AMD Turned a Chip Portfolio into a Platform Strategy
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. has reshaped the tech landscape by evolving from a CPU vendor to a comprehensive compute platform player, driving innovation across PCs, gaming consoles, and data centers. The company's strategy involves delivering high performance per watt and per dollar, leveraging chiplet-based designs, and building an open ecosystem to compete effectively against industry giants Intel and NVIDIA. This approach has transformed AMD into a vertically integrated provider of compute engines, positioning it as a central player in the future of computing infrastructure.

This facility focuses on foundational analog and embedded processors crucial for AI and automotive industries, utilizing 300mm wafers to reduce fabrication costs. The investment aims to secure the domestic supply chain for critical chips, with future expansion plans to produce over 100 million chips per day.

feed.title: Trump Blocks $2.9 Million HieFo-Emcore Chip Deal Over China-Linked National Security Concerns

The executive order mandates HieFo to divest all interests in Emcore's assets within 180 days, with the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) overseeing enforcement. This action is part of a broader U.S. strategy to curb China's access to advanced semiconductor technologies and maintain American technological superiority.

The article discusses the impact of AI demand on memory supply, with manufacturers diverting capacity to HBM, and anticipates upcoming U.S. jobs and inflation data as key catalysts for stock movement. Investors are closely watching for signals on memory contract pricing and HBM availability amidst debates about a potential "supercycle" in the memory market.

feed.title: CES 2026 AMD Keynote livestream: See it live

feed.title: CES 2026: Intel Core Ultra Series 3 Debuts as First Built on Intel 18A

These processors offer exceptional performance, graphics, and battery life, powering over 200 PC designs and are also certified for embedded and industrial use cases like robotics and smart cities. The new series includes Core Ultra X9 and X7 processors with integrated Intel Arc graphics, boasting significant improvements in multithread performance, gaming, and battery life.

feed.title: Watch the NVIDIA CES 2026 keynote with CEO Jensen Huang live here: AI, robotics updates and more

NVIDIA remains a key player in AI infrastructure, with its announcements closely watched by both tech enthusiasts and Wall Street investors.

feed.title: Entero's Grid AI Prepares for Major U.S. Expansion with New Leadership Appointments to Accelerate AI Infrastructure Strategy

This move coincides with significant growth in the AI data center industry, projected to reach $1 trillion globally by 2030, requiring advanced AI orchestration and energy optimization systems that are core to Grid AI's platform. The new leadership aims to enhance Grid AI's operational depth and market integration for large-scale AI deployments in the U.S.

feed.title: Supermicro Announces Support for Upcoming NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8 and Expanded Rack-Scale Manufacturing Capacity for Liquid-Cooled AI Solutions

Investors are watching for any insights into AI-driven demand and data-center investments from CES keynotes, though concerns remain about U.S. export controls impacting China-related demand and potential revenue losses for Applied Materials. The company's next earnings report on February 12th will provide crucial guidance on wafer-fab tool demand and margins.

feed.title: HP at CES 2026 — HP Reimagines the Desk for the Future of Work

feed.title: AMD unveils new AI chips at CES event in Las Vegas
authors: Max A. Cherney and Gnaneshwar Rajan
feed.summary: At the CES trade show in Las Vegas, AMD CEO Lisa Su announced new AI chips, including the MI455 processors for data centers and the MI440X for on-premise business use. Su also previewed the MI500, promising 1,000 times the performance of an older version, set to launch in 2027. While AMD aims to challenge Nvidia's dominance, OpenAI President Greg Brockman highlighted the critical role of AMD's chip advancements for AI computing needs.

feed.title: AMD Crashes CES 2026 With All-Out Offensive Against Nvidia, Touts ‘World’s Best AI Rack’ — And Retail Is Delighted
feed.summary: At CES 2026, AMD launched new chips including the MI440X and previewed the MI500 Series, intensifying its competition with Nvidia in the AI data center market. CEO Lisa Su unveiled Helios, an AI rack system she claimed was the "world's best," while also introducing new AI-powered PC chips and gaming processors. Retail investor sentiment on Stocktwits shifted to "extremely bullish" following the announcements, highlighting AMD's strong performance and future pipeline.

feed.title: AMD Expands AI Leadership Across Client, Graphics, and Software with New Ryzen, Ryzen AI, and AMD ROCm Announcements at CES 2026
feed.summary: At CES 2026, AMD unveiled a new generation of mobile and desktop processors, significantly expanding its AI capabilities across client computing. This includes the new Ryzen AI 400 Series for Copilot+ PCs, Ryzen AI Max+ processors, and the Ryzen AI PRO 400 Series for business laptops. AMD also introduced the Ryzen AI Halo developer platform and the Ryzen 7 9850X3D, the fastest gaming processor, alongside updates to its ROCm™ 7.2 software for seamless AI integration and FSR “Redstone” for enhanced gaming.

feed.title: AMD launches Zen 5-based Ryzen AI Embedded chips at CES
feed.summary: AMD has unveiled its new Ryzen AI Embedded P100 and X100 Series processors at CES 2026, designed to bring higher-performance, lower-latency artificial intelligence to embedded systems across various industries. These chips integrate Zen 5 CPU cores, RDNA 3.5 graphics, and XDNA 2 NPUs into a single system-on-chip, catering to the needs of next-generation automotive, industrial automation, and autonomous edge systems. The P100 series, ideal for infotainment systems, offers significant performance improvements and NPU capabilities for AI workloads, while the X100 series targets more demanding physical AI applications.

feed.title: New AMD Ryzen AI chips designed for car dashboards and factory robots
feed.summary: AMD announced its new Ryzen AI Embedded P100 and X100 processor families, specifically designed for AI-driven applications in automotive, industrial, and physical AI edge systems. These chips combine Zen 5 CPU cores, an RDNA 3.5 GPU with 35% faster rendering, and an XDNA 2 NPU capable of up to 50 TOPS for low-latency AI acceleration. The P100 series, with 4-6 cores, is sampling now with production expected in Q2 2026, while higher-core P100 and X100 variants will follow later in H1 2026.

feed.title: AMD maps an 'AI everywhere' future, from classrooms to robots
feed.summary: AMD announced its "AI everywhere" strategy at CES 2026, unveiling new products and partnerships to scale AI from cloud to edge. Key highlights include the "Helios" rack-scale platform for yotta-scale AI infrastructure, the new Instinct MI440X enterprise GPU, and a preview of the MI500 series targeting 1,000x performance increase. Additionally, AMD launched new Ryzen AI platforms for PCs and embedded applications and committed $150 million to AI education.

feed.title: Ceva Delivers Real-Time AI Acceleration on NXP's Processors for Software-Defined Vehicles

The integration supports applications like extended battery lifespan prediction, predictive maintenance, and in-cabin voice control, enhancing safety and intelligence in next-generation vehicles.

feed.title: AMD: CES 2026 revealed transformative AI innovations, partnerships, and hardware driving the next era of computing

feed.title: Broadridge completes acquisition of European fund services firm Acolin By Investing.com

This strategic move expands Broadridge's distribution and regulatory solutions for asset managers globally. The acquisition highlights Broadridge's ongoing growth, with steady revenue increases, and follows a strong fiscal Q1 2026 earnings report, although financial terms were not disclosed.

feed.title: Why SoFi Stock Dropped13% in December
feed.summary: SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ: SOFI) stock dropped 12% in December, despite the company's continued record-breaking customer growth and expansion into new financial products, including a stablecoin. The decline is attributed to its premium valuation and potentially falling cryptocurrency prices impacting adjacent stocks. While SoFi is performing well and is sustainably profitable, its high valuation (53 times trailing 12-month earnings) and exposure to the crypto market may have contributed to the recent stock movement.

feed.title: Nvidia: Full Steam Ahead With an Impressive Array of Announcements at CES 2026
feed.summary: Nvidia showcased its latest product innovations at CES 2026, including the Vera Rubin products set for release in the second half of 2026 which are expected to deliver five times the performance of current Blackwell products for certain AI inference tasks. The company also announced a full-stack autonomous driving solution to be integrated into the Mercedes-Benz CLA in Q1 2026, further solidifying its position in the physical AI market. Morningstar maintains its $240 fair value estimate for Nvidia, believing the stock to be undervalued despite recent AI bubble concerns.

feed.title: Himax and Vuzix to Introduce a Lightweight Prescription-Ready Optical Reference Design for AR Glasses at CES 2026
feed.summary: Himax and Vuzix have announced a new lightweight, prescription-ready optical reference design for AR glasses, set to debut at CES 2026. This design integrates Himax's ultra-compact HX7319FL LCoS microdisplay with Vuzix's high-efficiency waveguide technology, aiming to accelerate the commercialization of AR eyewear for both consumer and enterprise markets. The solution offers a compact, power-efficient platform supporting configurations like a 30° field of view and over 1000 nits brightness, designed to make AR glasses feel lighter and more comfortable for all-day wear.

feed.title: Intel Launches Next-Gen ‘Panther Lake’ Chips At CES
feed.summary: Intel launched its Panther Lake architecture-based chips, Intel Core Ultra Series 3, at CES in Las Vegas, marking the first time the company has used its new 18A manufacturing platform. These next-generation chips promise a 60 percent performance improvement over the previous Lunar Lake Series 2 and will power over 200 PC designs with enhanced compute, graphics, and battery life. Intel aims to regain market share against competitors like AMD and ARM-based chip makers by focusing on power efficiency, CPU and GPU performance, AI compute, and x86 app compatibility.

feed.title: Vistra Energy stock price target lowered to $230 at BMO Capital
feed.summary: BMO Capital has reduced its price target for Vistra Energy (NYSE:VST) to $230 from $245, while maintaining an Outperform rating. This adjustment follows Vistra's announcement of an agreement to acquire Cogentrix Energy for approximately $4 billion, expanding its natural gas generation capacity. Despite a recent 15.02% decline in VST shares over the last six months, BMO Capital has raised its adjusted EBITDA estimates for Vistra for 2027-2030, reflecting confidence in the company's strategic expansion and strengthened financial position.

feed.title: HP apresenta OmniBooks, OmniStudios e Chromebooks durante a CES 2026

These new devices integrate AI features, offer enhanced performance, and cater to various user profiles from students to creative professionals, focusing on mobility and productivity. Key highlights include the OmniBook Ultra 14 with a powerful NPU and long battery life, and the OmniStudio X 27 all-in-one for creators, alongside updated Chromebooks with Google AI integration.

feed.title: New driver-assist tech could make India’s trucks and buses safer by 2027
feed.summary: Aptiv PLC has secured a deal with a leading Indian commercial vehicle OEM to integrate its Gen 6 Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) into future trucks and buses in India, ahead of 2027 safety regulations. This turn-key solution, tailored for Indian road conditions, will cover 14 models and over 30 variants, enhancing safety through features like automatic emergency braking and blind spot warnings. The agreement highlights Aptiv's commitment to India's growing automotive sector and its investment in globalized yet localized technology.

feed.title: Railway Pension Investments Ltd Has $3.13 Million Holdings in PTC Inc. $PTC
feed.summary: Railway Pension Investments Ltd significantly reduced its stake in PTC Inc. by 91.5% in the third quarter, leaving it with 15,426 shares valued at $3.13 million. Despite this, other hedge funds increased their holdings. PTC reported strong Q3 earnings with $2.10 EPS and $1.76 billion in revenue, holding a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts with an average target price of $205.92.

feed.title: If Nvidia brings back the RTX 3060 it might spell bad news for PC gaming hardware
feed.summary: Nvidia might restart production of the RTX 3060 GPU, a move prompted by the current memory shortage impacting the PC hardware industry. While more GPU options could benefit consumers, this decision also suggests dire market conditions and raises concerns about Nvidia prioritizing AI accelerator production over gaming GPUs, as evidenced by a lack of gaming focus in a recent CES keynote. The original RTX 3060 used older GDDR6 memory and a different manufacturing process, which would free up resources currently constrained by newer GDDR7 production for RTX 50-series and Blackwell chips.

feed.title: Amkor Technology (AMKR) Hits 25-Year High on Rosy Prospects
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Inc. (AMKR) saw its stock reach a 25-year high after Needham & Company upgraded its price target by 35% to $50, citing Amkor's potential to become a leader in Chip on Wafer on Substrate (CoWoS) technology. This surge is driven by increasing demand for AI chips and an anticipated transition to Chip on Polymer on Substrate (CoPoS), which could lead Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd. to outsource CoWoS demand to Amkor.

feed.title: The Most Revealing Thing About Nvidia’s New Chip Comes Down to the Name
feed.summary: Nvidia revealed its next-generation AI platform, "Rubin," at CES, positioning itself as the indispensable infrastructure provider for the expanding AI landscape. The platform, a supercomputer designed for efficiency in AI workloads, emphasizes Nvidia's commitment to enabling advanced AI infrastructure. The name itself, "Rubin," is highlighted as more significant than technical specifications, subtly communicating Nvidia's deep ambitions in the AI sector.

feed.title: Earnings Estimates Moving Higher for Adeia (ADEA): Time to Buy?

feed.title: Ceva’s AI DSP powers NXP processors for software-defined vehicles By Investing.com
feed.summary: Ceva, Inc. announced that NXP Semiconductors has integrated Ceva's AI digital signal processor (DSP) technology into its S32Z2 and S32E2 real-time processors for software-defined vehicles. This integration will enable predictive analytics and intelligent control, supporting a market projected to grow significantly by 2030. Ceva's SensPro AI DSP aims to facilitate real-time decision-making and perception functions within next-generation automotive control modules, as revealed at CES 2026.

feed.title: AMD: AMD unveiled next-gen AI hardware and projected 5B daily AI users by 2030, driving industry growth
feed.summary: AMD unveiled its next-gen AI hardware, including the MI455X GPU and Ryzen AI 400 Series, and projected 5 billion daily AI users by 2030. The company highlighted rapid AI adoption and emphasized its open ecosystem, partnerships, and investments in education and research to maintain its leadership in AI innovation. This positions AMD to drive significant growth in the AI industry.

feed.title: AMD Showcases Growing AI Hardware Arsenal at CES2026
feed.summary: During her CES 2026 keynote, AMD CEO Lisa Su unveiled new AI chips and rack-scale computing systems, including Helios, designed to meet the soaring demand for continuous AI operations. AMD emphasized partnerships with companies like OpenAI and Luma AI, showcasing how their hardware supports complex, real-world AI applications beyond traditional training. The company also introduced new Ryzen AI processors for PCs, highlighting a strategic shift towards providing integrated, scalable AI infrastructure to compete with Nvidia.

feed.title: “AI is the most important technology of the last 50 years”: AMD CEO at CES
feed.summary: During the CES opening keynote, AMD Chair and CEO Dr. Lisa Su declared AI the most important technology of the last 50 years, emphasizing its potential to transform various aspects of life and the need for a 100x increase in global compute capacity. She unveiled new AI chips, including the MI455 and MI440X, highlighting collaborations with OpenAI, Luma AI, Liquid AI, and World Labs, and discussed AI's applications in healthcare and physical AI. The event also touched on the Genesis Mission, a public-private partnership aimed at leveraging AI for scientific discoveries, and AMD's investment in AI education programs.

feed.title: NVIDIA Reveals Why the Company Is “Immune” to Memory Shortages — and How The Firm Prepaid Inventory to Keep the AI Boom Running
feed.summary: NVIDIA claims to be "immune" to the current memory shortages affecting the industry, attributing this to early and massive investments in partners, including prepayments, to expand capacity. The company's direct purchasing of DRAM at a global scale and its ability to transform it into CoWoS supercomputers provide a significant advantage in managing its supply chain. While NVIDIA's AI division benefits from these strategies, its consumer gaming business, however, is feeling the pinch, experiencing product launch delays and necessitating the reintroduction of older GPU models.

feed.title: Cadence Launches Partner Ecosystem to Accelerate Chiplet Time to Market
feed.summary: Cadence has launched a Chiplet Spec-to-Packaged Parts ecosystem to reduce engineering complexity and accelerate time to market for customers developing chiplets for physical AI, data center, and high-performance computing applications. This initiative involves key partners like Arm and Samsung Foundry, and leverages Cadence's Physical AI chiplet platform with pre-integrated and pre-validated IP. The ecosystem aims to provide a low-risk path to advanced chiplet adoption, optimizing costs and offering customization flexibility through standards-compliant architectures.

feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets

These new offerings incorporate technologies from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD, delivering increased performance and energy efficiency for AI applications. The company aims to empower creators and developers with advanced desktop, edge, and AI PC solutions.

feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets

These systems are designed to bring enterprise-class AI performance to the client, edge, and consumer markets, featuring technologies from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD. The company aims to deliver unprecedented performance and energy efficiency for AI applications across various sectors, from research to personal use.

feed.title: Silicon Sovereignty: Texas Instruments’ SM1 Fab Leads the Charge in America’s Semiconductor Renaissance
feed.summary: Texas Instruments' SM1 fab in Sherman, Texas, has begun high-volume production of analog and embedded processing chips, marking a significant milestone for American technology under the CHIPS and Science Act. This facility aims to reduce fabrication costs, increase efficiency, and provide stable domestic hardware for the AI revolution and autonomous vehicles. The SM1 fab is a key part of the U.S. strategy to secure its technological lead and de-risk the hardware supply chain against geopolitical instability.

feed.title: Amkor Technology’s Unexpected Surge: Analyzing Market Impact

(NASDAQ: AMKR) experienced a significant surge, with its stock trading up by 9.87% due to advancements in semiconductor technologies, particularly in the AI wafer market. feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets

feed.title: Royal Bank, Telus back $130-million financing by quantum developer Photonic
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada and Telus Corp. have invested in a US$130-million equity financing round for Vancouver-based quantum computer developer Photonic Inc., alongside past backers like Microsoft Corp. and British Columbia Investment Management Corp. This investment, which values Photonic at "multiple billions" of dollars, reinforces Canada's position in the emerging quantum computing sector. Photonic uses a silicon-based approach for its quantum computers, which allows for modular expansion and less extreme cooling requirements compared to other technologies.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan stock jumps as copper hits a new record — what investors watch next
