This rally is driven by supply shocks and policy noise, though analysts have differing views on whether current copper prices are justified by market fundamentals. Investors are now watching for potential cooling in demand, a jump in supply, or shifts in U.S. trade policy that could impact the ongoing copper rally.

feed.title: AMD stock slips after CES AI chip push puts OpenAI deal back in focus
feed.summary: AMD shares fell despite the company showcasing new AI processors at CES, including the MI455 designed for data centers and the MI440X for on-premises use. The mixed market reaction suggests investors are looking for concrete sales growth from AMD's product roadmap, especially with upcoming U.S. labor reports that could influence interest rates and tech valuations. AMD is positioning itself as an alternative to Nvidia, particularly for enterprise buyers prioritizing on-premises AI deployment, though it faces challenges in gaining market share against Nvidia's established ecosystem.

feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets
feed.summary: Supermicro is showcasing its new Super AI Station and a portfolio of products designed for AI developers, startups, and education professionals at CES. These systems leverage the latest technologies from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD to bring enterprise-class AI performance to desktop, edge, and consumer markets, offering unprecedented power and energy efficiency for a wide range of AI applications. The offerings include a liquid-cooled desktop super-chip, Intel Xeon 6 SoC-powered workstations, and AMD CPU-based slim AI PCs, alongside specialized edge AI systems.

feed.title: Does Lattice Semiconductor’s (LSCC) Edge-AI Focus Reveal a Durable Competitive Moat in Low-Power FPGAs?
feed.summary: Lattice Semiconductor is positioning its low-power FPGAs at the center of AI-driven applications, highlighted by its presence at the International VLSID Conference and the enhanced sensAI edge-AI stack. While this focus aligns with the recent AI-driven rally, investors should be aware of rising competition and alternative silicon options. The company's narrative projects significant revenue and earnings growth, leading to a fair value aligned with its current price.

feed.title: CES 2026: HP Launches An AI PC In A Keyboard With EliteBoard G1a
feed.summary: HP has unveiled the EliteBoard G1a at CES 2026, an innovative AI PC integrated into a keyboard form factor. This design offers display flexibility and workspace customization, powered by an AMD Ryzen AI 300 Series chip with over 50 TOPS of NPU performance. Two versions will be available, one with a detachable cable and battery for display sharing, and a stationary version with a lockable cable.

feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its portfolio with new AI-powered systems for client, edge, and consumer markets, showcased in Las Vegas. These new products include the Super AI Station, Supermicro SYS-542T-2R, and Supermicro AI PC, which integrate NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD technologies to deliver high performance and energy efficiency for AI applications. The company aims to meet the evolving demands of AI developers, startups, higher education, and professionals with these innovations.

feed.title: Supermicro Announces Support for Upcoming NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8 and Expanded Rack-Scale Manufacturing Capacity for Liquid-Cooled AI Solutions
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its manufacturing capacity and liquid-cooling capabilities in collaboration with NVIDIA to deliver data center-scale solutions optimized for NVIDIA's forthcoming Vera Rubin and Rubin platforms. This initiative will enable the rapid deployment of flagship NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 and NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8 systems, leveraging Supermicro's Data Center Building Block Solutions approach. The company aims to provide hyperscalers and enterprises with advanced AI infrastructure featuring industry-leading liquid-cooling technology for enhanced speed, efficiency, and reliability.

feed.title: A Supermicro leva desempenho de IA em nível empresarial para os mercados corporativo, de edge e consumidor
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its AI solutions portfolio to corporate, edge, and consumer markets with new high-performance systems based on NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD technologies. This includes the Super AI Station with NVIDIA GB200 Grace Blackwell, AI PCs with AMD CPUs, and Edge AI Systems with AMD EPYC 4005 and Intel Core Ultra Series 3 processors. The company aims to provide unprecedented performance and power efficiency for AI development and applications across various sectors.

feed.title: A Supermicro leva desempenho de IA em nível empresarial para os mercados corporativo, de edge e consumidor
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its portfolio of edge solutions with advanced platforms based on NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD, bringing enterprise-level AI performance to corporate, edge, and consumer markets. The company is unveiling its new Super AI Station and other systems at CES, designed for AI developers, startups, and educational institutions, offering unprecedented performance and energy efficiency. These solutions aim to address the growing demand for AI applications by providing powerful, localized computing capabilities that overcome the limitations of traditional server infrastructures and cloud-based AI services.

feed.title: Agencia de Comunicacão - A Supermicro anuncia suporte às futuras plataformas NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 e HGX Rubin NVL8, além de expandir sua capacidade de fabricação em escala de rack para soluções de IA com resfriamento a líquido.
feed.summary: Supermicro announced expanded manufacturing capacity and liquid cooling capabilities to support NVIDIA's upcoming Vera Rubin NVL72 and HGX Rubin NVL8 platforms. The company's Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) are designed for rapid deployment of next-generation AI infrastructure, offering advanced liquid cooling and optimized performance for AI and HPC workloads. This collaboration aims to provide hyperscalers and enterprises with scalable, efficient, and reliable AI infrastructure solutions.

feed.title: Alphabet (Google) Class C stock (GOOG) slips as Texas privacy fee award and CES put Waymo in focus
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) dipped by 0.7% due to a $156 million legal-fee award for a Texas privacy settlement and investor focus on CES developments. The company is under scrutiny for legal and regulatory costs, even as the market looks for AI-driven growth. Meanwhile, CES highlights AI chip advancements and autonomous driving, with Waymo remaining a key industry player.

feed.title: AMD stock tumbles sharply: why chip giant is facing profit-taking after 2025 rally
feed.summary: AMD stock experienced a sharp decline on Tuesday due to profit-taking following an exceptional 97% surge in 2025. The selloff was exacerbated by technical resistance, competitive pressure from Intel's new chips unveiled at CES, and mixed analyst sentiment regarding AMD's growth targets and market positioning against Nvidia.

feed.title: Stock Market Live January 6, 2026: S&P 500 (SPY) Nearing All-Time Highs
feed.summary: The S&P 500 is approaching all-time highs on January 6, 2026, driven by strong performance in AI-related stocks like Nvidia and Palantir. Nvidia announced its next-generation Vera Rubin superchip at CES 2026, while Palantir received a buy rating and a $223 price target from Truist, further boosting investor confidence in the AI sector's continued growth. Additionally, Deutsche Bank upgraded Shake Shack to a buy rating, noting attractive valuation and growth potential.

feed.title: Synopsys Showcases Vision For AI-Driven, Software-Defined Automotive Engineering at CES 2026
feed.summary: Synopsys is presenting its AI-driven and software-defined engineering solutions at CES 2026 to address the challenge of accelerating automotive innovation while reducing costs and complexity. The company's solutions, from system-level simulation to semiconductor design, aim to virtualize silicon and software development, predict performance, and optimize reliability for automakers and suppliers. Key highlights include support for the FIA's safety standards, integration of Samsung ISOCELL Auto 1H1 into Ansys AVxcelerate Sensors, and Virtualizer Developer Kits for faster time to market.

feed.title: Emerging Risk: Export Controls Compliance in New Technologies
feed.summary: Companies at the forefront of AI, semiconductors, and large-scale data infrastructure face increasing responsibility to comply with export controls, which are now being used to protect national security. The evolving regulatory landscape, new threats like diversion risks and insider threats, and the complexities of global supply chains necessitate agile and adaptable compliance programs. A sound, risk-based export controls compliance program is crucial for navigating this fractured regulatory environment and protecting both company interests and national security.

feed.title: Microchip Stock Surges. Texas Instruments and Other Chip Makers Are Rising, Too.
feed.summary: Microchip Technology's stock surged after the company raised its sales guidance for the current quarter, indicating plans to boost production. This positive movement is also impacting other major chip manufacturers like Texas Instruments and Qualcomm, who are seeing their stocks rise in correlation.

feed.title: AMD to Report Fiscal Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) announced it will release its fiscal fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Tuesday, February 3, 2026, after market close, followed by a conference call. Additionally, Mark Papermaster, EVP and CTO, will present at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 3, 2026. Both events will be webcast on AMD's Investor Relations website.

feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI) is expanding its portfolio with new high-performance Super AI Stations designed for AI/ML, HPC, Cloud, Storage, and 5G/Edge markets. These new products aim to bring enterprise-class AI performance to client, edge, and consumer markets, featuring technologies from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD. The offerings include a liquid-cooled Super AI Station for AI models, an Intel Xeon 6 SoC workstation for VDI services, AMD CPU-powered AI PCs, and various edge AI systems for virtualized workloads.

feed.title: Intel is building a handheld gaming platform including a dedicated chip
feed.summary: Intel is developing a new handheld gaming platform that includes a dedicated chip, building on its Intel Core Series 3 processors (Panther Lake). This move signifies Intel's intent to deepen its presence in the gaming hardware market, currently dominated by AMD. The company plans to release more details about these new products later in the year, expanding beyond its traditional PC gaming chip focus.

feed.title: Linkhome AI Introduces Home Humanoid and Quadruped Robots
feed.summary: Linkhome AI has introduced new product lines of home humanoid and quadruped robots, currently under development for residential and multi-scenario applications. These robots are designed for companionship, household assistance, environment inspection, and safety monitoring, integrating AI and hardware-software architecture for an enhanced intelligent living ecosystem. This initiative expands Linkhome AI's artificial intelligence capabilities from digital platforms into the physical world, aiming to provide comprehensive smart living solutions throughout the housing lifecycle.

feed.title: AMD CEO says AI demand is 'going through the roof' as costs climb
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su stated that demand for AI is "going through the roof" and that the cost of individual AI chips can be tens of thousands of dollars. She explained that AMD's AI systems bundle dozens of these high-end chips and companies need massive investments in computing power to remain competitive in the AI market. Su projected that the world will need "10 yottaflops" of computing power in the coming years to keep up with AI's rapid growth.

feed.title: AMD to Report Fiscal Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: AMD announced it will report its fiscal fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Tuesday, February 3, 2026, followed by a conference call. Additionally, Mark Papermaster will present at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 3, 2026. Both events will be accessible via webcast on the AMD Investor Relations website.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Stock Is Quietly Popping Off – Is TI the Boring-Looking Chip King You Actually Need to Own?
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) is presented as a quiet, long-term investment that leverages its dominance in analog chips and embedded processing to generate substantial free cash flow and consistent dividend increases. While not as flashy as AI-focused chip stocks, TI offers stability and reliable returns for investors seeking passive income and durability, rather than volatile, high-hype plays. The article suggests TXN is worth considering for a long-term portfolio due to its solid business model and focus on essential, deeply embedded technologies.

feed.title: WeRide's Robotaxi GXR becomes world's first Robotaxi to adopt NVIDIA DRIVE Thor X chip
feed.summary: WeRide's Robotaxi GXR has become the world's first Robotaxi to be powered by NVIDIA's DRIVE Thor X chip, entering fully driverless commercial operations in China and the UAE. This achievement highlights WeRide's technical prowess and international deployment, leveraging NVIDIA's L4 autonomous driving ecosystem. The GXR, equipped with the HPC 3.0 computing platform, operates in multiple cities and aims for large-scale global deployment with thousands of Robotaxis by 2030.

feed.title: AMD stock slips after CES AI chip push as Feb. 3 earnings comes into view
feed.summary: AMD shares fell 3.1% after showcasing new AI products at CES, putting its AI chip roadmap in focus against competitors like Nvidia and Intel. The company announced its next earnings report for February 3rd, and investors are closely watching for proof that new accelerator chips are translating into sustained sales. The broader market rose, but AMD faces pressure to show faster traction in AI deployments given Nvidia's continued rapid advancements.

feed.title: What's the 'Next Big Thing' in AI? Here's What Nvidia and AMD Execs Say It Could Be
feed.summary: According to AMD CEO Lisa Su and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, the next major phase of the AI boom will likely involve applications in the physical world, specifically "physical AI" powering autonomous machines like humanoid robots and self-driving cars. Both companies are strategically focusing on this area, with Nvidia asserting that a "ChatGPT moment" for robotics has already arrived. This shift represents a significant growth opportunity for investors as AI technologies expand into tangible real-world applications.

feed.title: John Deere's Deanna Kovar and the Road From Precision Farming to Autonomous Industry
feed.summary: John Deere demonstrated its autonomous heavy machinery at CES 2026, showcasing systems already operating in agriculture and expanding into construction. Deanna Kovar, President of Worldwide Agriculture and Turf for Production and Precision Agriculture, leads this initiative, emphasizing autonomy, policy, and infrastructure rooted in the practical needs of operators. The company's strategy focuses on repeatability, measurement, and availability, with plans to deploy battery-electric autonomous tractors by 2026.

feed.title: 3 major takeaways from Nvidia Live at CES 2026
feed.summary: At CES 2026, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang underscored the company's ambition to dominate all AI-driven domains, moving beyond consumer GPUs. The keynote highlighted "physical AI" for robotics and autonomous vehicles, showcasing the Cosmos and Alpamayo models, and revealing plans for a robotaxi service by 2027. Nvidia also focused on its Rubin AI platform for data centers and its strategy of providing open AI models to cement its role as the foundational technology for an AI-powered world.

feed.title: Freshworks’ Lawrence Philippa sells $8881 in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Freshworks' Chief Accounting Officer, Lawrence Philippa, sold 765 shares of Class A Common Stock for $8,881 and disposed of an additional 2,333 shares for $27,062 to cover tax obligations. Concurrently, he acquired 120,870 shares through Restricted Stock Units. This news follows Freshworks' acquisition of FireHydrant and positive analyst ratings from Needham and UBS, based on the company's strong demand for Freshservice and AI developments.

feed.title: SentinelOne CEO Weingarten sells shares worth $875k By Investing.com
feed.summary: SentinelOne CEO Tomer Weingarten sold 57,941 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $875,564 on January 2nd and 6th, 2026, executed under a 10b5-1 trading plan. The sales occurred while SentinelOne's stock traded near its 52-week low. Despite positive Q3 2026 earnings, analyst opinions are mixed, with some downgrades due to management changes, while others maintain positive ratings citing growth outlook and valuation.

feed.title: Supermicro Brings Enterprise-Class AI Performance to the Client, Edge, and Consumer Markets
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its portfolio with new AI-powered systems for client, edge, and consumer markets, featuring technologies from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD. The company announced its Super AI Station for high-performance AI development, along with new workstations, AI PCs, and compact edge AI systems. These innovations aim to deliver enterprise-class AI capabilities to a broader range of users, including creators, developers, startups, and higher education.

feed.title: GigCapital7 progresses financing efforts for Hadron merger
feed.summary: GigCapital7 Corp. is actively pursuing financing to support its proposed business combination with Hadron Energy, Inc. These efforts include engaging with investors for private capital raises through simple agreements for future equity and potential PIPE financing. Both companies have filed a registration statement and are working towards shareholder approval for the merger.

feed.title: Nexxen International reduces board size to six directors
feed.summary: Nexxen International Ltd. announced a reduction in its board size to six directors, including five non-executive members, following the departure of Joanna Parnell and Norm Johnston. This move is part of an ongoing review to align with Nasdaq-listed company governance standards. Despite a recent stock price decline, the advertising technology company maintains strong financial fundamentals and is seen as undervalued by InvestingPro.

feed.title: NVDA Crushes AMD? Morgan Stanley’s Post-CES AI Winner
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore has identified Nvidia (NVDA) as the clear AI winner over Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) following CES 2026. Moore was significantly more impressed by Nvidia's new Rubin platform, which he believes will "raise the bar for performance" in AI hardware, automotive, and robotics. Conversely, AMD offered little new information on its MI450 series, leading Moore to conclude that its recent gains are more due to overall AI demand rather than a distinct competitive advantage.

feed.title: AMD aprofunda o impulso em IA corporativa com novos chips de data center
feed.summary: AMD lançou o novo chip MI440X direcionado a data centers corporativos menores, visando a IA local e reduzindo a dependência de grandes provedores de nuvem. A empresa também revelou o acelerador MI455X para cargas de trabalho de IA exigentes e anunciou planos para a série MI500 em 2027, prometendo ganhos de desempenho significativos. Este movimento visa diminuir a lacuna com a Nvidia no mercado de chips de IA, com a CEO Lisa Su enfatizando a contínua e forte demanda por poder de computação de IA.

feed.title: Ações da AMD caem após destaque para chips de IA na CES reacender foco em acordo com a OpenAI
feed.summary: As ações da AMD caíram cerca de 1,3% após a CES, apesar de a empresa ter apresentado novos processadores de IA para data centers e clientes corporativos, incluindo uma plataforma em colaboração com a OpenAI. Os investidores buscam provas concretas de crescimento de vendas, enquanto a Nvidia também revelou sua plataforma de próxima geração. A AMD busca se posicionar como uma alternativa à Nvidia, focando em soluções de IA para dados regulados e inferência sensível à latência, mas enfrenta o desafio da consolidação do software da Nvidia e possíveis restrições na cadeia de suprimentos.

feed.title: PENG Q1 Deep Dive: Enterprise AI Uptake and Memory Growth Offset LED Weakness
feed.summary: Penguin Solutions (NASDAQ: PENG) reported Q4 CY2025 results that surpassed revenue expectations, with flat year-on-year sales but an adjusted EPS beat. The company saw strong enterprise AI adoption and memory growth, which helped offset ongoing weaknesses in its LED segment. Management highlighted a shift from hyperscale to broader enterprise AI deployments and anticipates continued growth in these areas, along with increased memory demand, in the coming quarters.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on These Technology Stocks: Unusual Machines Inc. (UMAC), CGI (GIB)
feed.summary: This article highlights bullish sentiments from financial analysts on three technology stocks: Unusual Machines Inc. (UMAC), CGI (GIB), and Broadcom (AVGO). Unusual Machines Inc. received a "Strong Buy" consensus with an average price target of $18.67. CGI was rated a "Moderate Buy" with a C$130.06 average price target, and Broadcom received a "Strong Buy" with an average price target of $461.42.

feed.title: Tectonic Advisors LLC Has $11.70 Million Stock Holdings in Broadcom Inc. $AVGO
feed.summary: Tectonic Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Broadcom Inc. by 33.3% in the third quarter, now holding 35,461 shares valued at $11.70 million. Despite this, Broadcom reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst expectations, and has received a consensus "Buy" rating with an average target price of $436.89 from analysts. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.65 per share, although insiders have recently sold a significant amount of shares.

feed.title: HP Brings AMD Powered Keyboard Computer and New 4K Office Monitor with Thunderbolt 4 to CES
feed.summary: HP has unveiled the EliteBoard G1a Next Gen AI PC, a keyboard that houses a full AI PC, and the HP Series 7 Pro 4K Monitor at CES 2026. The EliteBoard G1a features an AMD Ryzen AI 300 Series processor, offering next-gen AI performance in a compact form factor. The accompanying 4K monitor provides high visual fidelity and robust connectivity with Thunderbolt 4, catering to demanding professional users.

feed.title: HP lança notebook EliteBook X G2 com opções de chips IA da Intel, AMD e Qualcomm
feed.summary: HP announced the EliteBook X G2 series at CES 2026, featuring new AI-powered notebooks designed for productivity and business leadership. The line offers configurations with Intel, AMD, and Qualcomm AI chips, an ultrafine design with recycled aluminum, and advanced features like an OLED Tandem 3K display and up to 29 hours of battery life. The company emphasized the integration of AI to alleviate work pressure, reflecting findings from its 2025 Work Relationship Index.

feed.title: AMD's "AI Everywhere, for Everyone" Push: Is the Stock Ready to Rally?
feed.summary: AMD announced its "AI Everywhere, for Everyone" strategy at CES 2026, unveiling new technologies like the Helios rack-scale platform and Instinct MI400 series to bolster its AI presence. Despite strong AI initiatives and an expanding portfolio, the company faces stiff competition from NVIDIA and Intel, and its stock currently exhibits a stretched valuation. While strong growth is anticipated in data center AI revenues, the current Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) suggests investors might wait for a better entry point.

feed.title: Louisiana State Employees Retirement System Purchases 3,638 Shares of Synopsys, Inc. $SNPS
feed.summary: The Louisiana State Employees Retirement System increased its holding in Synopsys, Inc. by 18.7% during the third quarter, adding 3,638 shares to now own 23,138 shares valued at $11.42 million. Synopsys reported strong quarterly results with EPS beating estimates and revenue up 37.8% year-over-year, alongside positive guidance for FY2026 and Q1 2026. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Synopsys, with a $562.13 average price target.

feed.title: Arteris (NASDAQ:AIP) COO Sells $83,012.85 in Stock
feed.summary: Arteris (NASDAQ:AIP) COO Laurent Moll sold 5,373 shares of the company's stock on January 2nd for $83,012.85, reducing his stake by 2.09% to 251,993 shares. Following the sale, Arteris stock saw an 8.3% increase to $17.58, with analysts maintaining a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average price target of $17.50. The company, which specializes in on-chip interconnect solutions, recently reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings and has seen varied analyst ratings and increased hedge fund activity.

feed.title: CareDx and 10x Genomics partner on transplant rejection research By Investing.com
feed.summary: CareDx and 10x Genomics are collaborating on ImmuneScape, a multiomics research initiative focused on transplant organ rejection using advanced single-cell platforms. CareDx, boasting a strong financial position, aims to leverage its clinical expertise with 10x Genomics' technologies to map immune mechanisms underlying rejection and develop future clinical diagnostics. This partnership builds on CareDx's recent earnings beat and new data on its HeartCare molecular testing, despite a recent downgrade by Craig-Hallum for its stock.

feed.title: Kyndryl Announces Retirement of Chief Human Resources Officer
feed.summary: Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. announced that its Chief Human Resources Officer, Maryjo Charbonnier, will retire on March 31, 2026, after more than four years with the company. She will remain as an Executive Advisor until August 31, 2026, to ensure a smooth transition. Analysts currently rate Kyndryl (KD) stock as a Buy with a $30.00 price target.

feed.title: Radware Sets February 11 Release Date for Q4 and Full-Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Radware (RDWR) announced that it will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on February 11, 2026. The company's management will host a conference call on the same day to discuss the results and provide an outlook for Q1 2026. An analyst rated the stock as "Hold" with a $25.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, assessed RDWR as "Neutral," citing strong financials and growth in cloud security against bearish technical indicators and high valuation.

feed.title: Securian Asset Management Inc. Sells 16,674 Shares of Intel Corporation $INTC
feed.summary: Securian Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in Intel Corporation by 7.3% in the third quarter, selling 16,674 shares and now holding 211,384 shares valued at $7.09 million. This adjustment comes as Intel unveils its new Core Ultra Series 3 processors, attracting OEM design wins and bolstering its manufacturing credibility. Despite mixed analyst ratings, with a consensus "Reduce" and a target price of $35.88, Intel's stock is currently trading around $40, reflecting ongoing market volatility and recent positive product news.

feed.title: What's Going On With Intel Stock Wednesday? - Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), Intel (NASDAQ:INTC)
feed.summary: Intel and AMD are intensifying their rivalry, particularly in the gaming and AI markets. Intel is developing a new Panther Lake processor and platform specifically for handheld gaming devices, aiming to challenge AMD's dominance in this sector. Meanwhile, AMD continues to gain market share in PC gaming and sees strong investor confidence due to its AI partnerships and product pipeline.

feed.title: Intel Stock Jumps as Management Teases New Gaming Chips
feed.summary: Intel (INTC) shares rose about 7% after an executive announced that the company is developing a new processor and platform specifically for gaming, combining hardware and software. This move signifies Intel's renewed focus on the gaming chip market, aiming to compete more strongly with AMD and Nvidia. Investors responded positively to the news, hoping it will drive growth in the high-demand gaming hardware sector.

feed.title: Apollo Backs $5.4 Billion Valor and xAI Data Center Compute Infrastructure Transaction with $3.5 Billion Capital Solution
feed.summary: Apollo-managed funds have led a $3.5 billion capital solution for Valor Compute Infrastructure (VCI) to support its $5.4 billion acquisition and lease of data center compute infrastructure, including NVIDIA GB200 GPUs, to xAI Corp. This financing is structured as a triple net lease and will power one of the world’s most powerful compute clusters for xAI's Grok model training and development. The deal highlights Apollo's role in funding critical AI infrastructure alongside partners like Valor and NVIDIA.

feed.title: Hp Inc stock hits 52-week low at $21.20 By Investing.com
feed.summary: HP Inc.'s stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $21.20, reflecting a 36.53% decrease over the past year due to various market challenges, including rising memory prices. This decline comes despite the company's recent launch of new AI-era business laptops and a private placement by HPQ Silicon Inc. Analysts have reiterated cautious ratings and lowered price targets for HP Inc. due to concerns over memory inflation.

feed.title: AMD's "AI Everywhere, for Everyone" Push: Is the Stock Ready to Rally?
feed.summary: AMD outlined its "AI Everywhere, for Everyone" strategy at CES 2026, introducing its Helios rack-scale platform and new Instinct MI400 and Ryzen AI 400 series processors to expand its footprint in AI data centers, HPC, and AI PCs. While AMD's shares have significantly outperformed the sector and competitors like Broadcom and NVIDIA over the past year, its valuation is currently stretched, and it faces stiff competition from NVIDIA and Intel in the AI market. Despite strong forecasted revenue growth driven by its data center prospects, AMD holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) due to its high valuation and competitive landscape.

feed.title: AMD stock slips in regular trade as CES buzz fades and Feb. 3 earnings loom
feed.summary: AMD shares fell about 2% in morning trade, lagging some chip peers, as the buzz from CES fades and investors look ahead to quarterly results on Feb. 3. Despite AMD showcasing new AI hardware at CES, an analyst noted the event didn't alter the debate around the stock, keeping focus on future MI455 ramp and OpenAI as an anchor customer. Macroeconomic data, including softer labor demand, also impacted rate-sensitive tech stocks.

feed.title: Hp Inc stock hits 52-week low at $21.20
feed.summary: HP Inc.'s stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $21.20, reflecting a 36.53% decline over the past year due to market challenges. The company has recently launched new AI-era laptops and secured $1 million in private placement funding, but analysts have reiterated cautious ratings and lowered price targets due to concerns over rising memory prices impacting future earnings.

feed.title: Global Mofy AI Takes 51% Stake in New U.S. Subsidiary Eaglepoint AI to Build Global Data and Training Hub
feed.summary: Global Mofy AI has acquired a 51% controlling stake in Eaglepoint AI Inc. for $100,000, establishing a new U.S. subsidiary aimed at building a global data and training hub. Eaglepoint AI will focus on data labeling, governance, and AI model training using its proprietary workflow tool, Aquila. This strategic move intends to create a scalable, cost-effective data supply chain and strengthen Global Mofy AI's position in enterprise AI services by leveraging African tech talent.

feed.title: Strategy Inc stock rises after MSCI drops plan to kick bitcoin-treasury firms out of indexes
feed.summary: Strategy Inc. (MSTR.O) saw its stock rise after MSCI announced it would not remove companies holding significant digital assets from its indexes, alleviating a major technical risk. While this offers a reprieve, analysts note that the issue may only be postponed, and the broader review could still lead to reclassification for firms like Strategy, which holds substantial bitcoin on its balance sheet. The company recently purchased more bitcoin and reported a significant Q4 unrealized loss related to crypto prices.

feed.title: Lam Research stock slips after two-day rally as traders weigh fresh Buy call and CES headlines
feed.summary: Lam Research (LRCX) shares fell 2.5% Wednesday, paring gains from a two-day rally, despite Aletheia Capital initiating coverage with a Buy rating and a $260 price target. The stock's performance reflects investor focus on chipmaking spend indicators and the broader semiconductor equipment sector's reaction to CES headlines and analyst attention ahead of earnings season. Other chip-related stocks like Applied Materials and KLA also saw declines, while Nvidia rose following its CEO's CES keynote.

feed.title: Aerospace Play Woodward Leads Select Group Of 8 Onto Best Stock Lists
feed.summary: Aerospace/defense stock Woodward (WWD) reached a record high and joined the Big Cap 20 list. Other top performers midweek included gold stocks Newmont (NEM) and OR Royalties (OR), and Eli Lilly (LLY), which also hit an all-time high. Highly rated biotech Septerna (SEPN) was also highlighted.

feed.title: Key facts: AMD Stock Drops 20% Amid AI Market Competition; TSMC Key Supplier
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has seen a 20% decline in its stock price, dropping to $215, forming an "island reversal" pattern. This decrease occurs amidst growing competition in the AI chip market, where AMD remains a key player, leveraging TSMC as its primary supplier alongside competitors like Nvidia and Broadcom.

feed.title: Key facts: Broadcom faces chip demand concerns; key AI competitor status
feed.summary: Broadcom is facing potential challenges as large tech firms may cut capital investments, which could lead to reduced chip demand. Despite these concerns, Broadcom remains a key competitor in the AI chip manufacturing sector, leveraging TSMC's market dominance alongside Nvidia and AMD.

feed.title: United Microelectronics Is Quietly Owning The Chip Wars – Are You Sleeping On This Stock?
feed.summary: The article highlights United Microelectronics (UMC) as a potentially overlooked investment in the semiconductor industry, contrasting it with well-known giants like Nvidia and TSMC. UMC specializes in mature manufacturing nodes for essential everyday electronics, offering stability and value for long-term investors. It is presented as a "quietly smart" alternative to more volatile, hype-driven tech stocks.

feed.title: Kaiser Aluminum Announces Executive Sales and Marketing Transition
feed.summary: Kaiser Aluminum has announced a planned executive leadership transition in its sales and marketing organization. Effective January 1, 2026, Hugh J. Barger, III will become Executive Vice President – Sales and Marketing, succeeding Blain A. Tiffany, who will transition to an Advisor to the CEO role until his retirement at the end of 2026. This move is part of the company's succession planning, ensuring a smooth handover of commercial leadership.

feed.title: Arteris (AIP) VP Alpern sells shares for $112,936
feed.summary: Arteris (AIP) VP and General Counsel Paul L. Alpern sold 6,818 common stock shares for $112,936 on January 5th and 6th, 2026, and also exercised options to acquire 2,500 shares. This transaction occurred as the stock trades near its 52-week high, despite analysis suggesting it is overvalued. The company recently reported strong third-quarter earnings, an 18% revenue increase, and announced the acquisition of Cycuity, Inc.

feed.title: Nvidia lança projeto de IA que promete revolucionar veículos autônomos
feed.summary: Nvidia has launched a new project called Alpamayo, focused on physical Artificial Intelligence, which aims to revolutionize autonomous vehicles and advanced robotics. The project includes open-source AI models, simulation tools, and datasets designed to train machines to understand complex scenarios and make safe, predictable decisions. This initiative positions Nvidia to lead the next phase of AI, moving beyond data analysis to allow machines to comprehend, decide, and act in the physical world.

feed.title: Aehr Announces Over $5.5 Million in Sonoma Ultra-High-Power System Orders to Test and Burn-in AI Processors; Introduces New Fully-Automated Sonoma for Next-Generation AI Applications
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems announced over $5.5 million in orders for its Sonoma ultra-high-power packaged-part burn-in systems from leading AI companies and a Bay Area lab. These systems are designed to test and burn-in next-generation CPUs, GPUs, and high-performance networking processors for AI and data center applications, with individual devices consuming up to 2000W. The company also introduced a new fully automated Sonoma platform, which provides seamless transition from engineering qualification to high-volume production, offering hands-free operation, continuous-flow burn-in, and the lowest total cost of ownership.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics transition to 18-nanometer technology is shaping the next generation of STM32V8 microcontrollers
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has unveiled the STM32V8, the world's first microcontroller using 18-nanometer FD-SOI technology, marking a significant advancement in MCU performance. This new architecture, combining 18 nm FD-SOI with phase-change memory, delivers higher performance, better power efficiency, and enhanced reliability compared to previous generations. The STM32V8, powered by the Arm Cortex-M85 core, operates at up to 800 MHz and is designed for demanding applications such as industrial automation, space technology, and Edge AI, supported by STMicroelectronics's expanding STM32 AI Model Zoo.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Notes That Lam Research Is One of the Stocks He Likes “So Much”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has expressed strong optimism for Lam Research Corporation (NASDAQ: LRCX), calling it a stock he likes "so much" and highlighting its role as a semiconductor capital equipment maker. He emphasized that memory companies rely on Lam Research for increased capacity and advised against selling the stock, even after significant gains. Cramer considers LRCX to be critical intellectual property within the semiconductor capital equipment sector.

feed.title: GraniteShares Announces Weekly Distributions for its YieldBOOST ETFs: COYY, TSYY, NVYY, XBTY, AMYY, TQQY, AZYY, YSPY, MTYY, PLYY, HOYY, SMYY, BBYY, FBYY, IOYY, MAAY, NUGY, SEMY, QBY, RGYY, HMYY and RTYY
feed.summary: GraniteShares has announced the weekly distributions for its YieldBOOST ETFs, including a wide array of ticker symbols such as COYY, TSYY, and NVYY, among many others. The announcement details the distribution per share, distribution rate, 30-day SEC yield, and return of capital (ROC) for each ETF. Investors are cautioned that distributions are not guaranteed and past performance does not indicate future results, with associated risks outlined.

feed.title: Nvidia and Siemens reveal fusion energy partnership at CES 2026
feed.summary: At CES 2026, Siemens President and CEO Roland Busch, joined by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, announced an expanded partnership focusing on an AI-driven industrial revolution to transform manufacturing and supply chain management. This collaboration includes a significant initiative with Commonwealth Fusion Systems to use AI the development of fusion energy; the companies will create a "digital twin" of the SPARC prototype fusion power plant to accelerate research. Other CES highlights included Razer's AI-powered gaming prototypes, Oshkosh Corporation’s autonomous airport robots, Roborock's stair-climbing vacuum, and Withings' upgraded Body Scan 2 smart scale.

feed.title: AMD (AMD): AI Growth Strategy, Server Shipments Surge, and Analyst Ratings
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMD) is highlighted as a must-buy AI stock due to its growth strategy, spurred by Micron's upbeat outlook for the AI market and server shipments. Analysts, despite some near-term challenges for AI infrastructure, remain optimistic about AMD's future and its market share goals in the server CPU space. Truist maintained its "Buying" rating for AMD, adjusting its price target slightly.

feed.title: Fukushima, Tempus AI COO, sells $321k in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Ryan Fukushima, COO of Tempus AI, Inc. (NASDAQ:TEM), sold 5,000 shares of Class A Common Stock for $321,300 on January 5, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The sale occurred after an 18% stock gain in the preceding week, with Tempus AI's stock showing a strong 79% return over the last year despite InvestingPro analysis suggesting it is overvalued. The company recently reported better-than-expected Q3 2025 earnings and announced a research partnership with the Institute for Follicular Lymphoma Innovation (IFLI).

feed.title: Babcock & Wilcox Selects Siemens Energy to Supply Steam Turbine Generator Sets for Applied Digital Data Center Power Project
feed.summary: Babcock & Wilcox (B&W) has chosen Siemens Energy to supply steam turbine generator sets for its project to provide one gigawatt of power for an Applied Digital AI Factory. This partnership aims to deliver reliable power for the rapidly growing AI Data Center market by the end of 2028. The collaboration leverages Siemens Energy's advanced turbine technology with B&W's proven boiler systems to meet an accelerated schedule.

feed.title: Here's What Lifted Oracle (ORCL) in Q3
feed.summary: Oracle Corporation's strong Q3 performance was driven by its rapidly expanding cloud business and a significant increase in its order backlog. The company secured multi-billion dollar deals with major tech entities like OpenAI, xAI, Meta, Nvidia, and AMD, leading to an almost four-fold increase in its backlog over the past year. Despite the fund's overall underperformance compared to the Russell 1000 due to its focus on lower-risk, high-quality factors, Oracle's contribution was a highlight.

feed.title: Strategic Planning Group LLC Sells 9,681 Shares of Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX
feed.summary: Strategic Planning Group LLC has reduced its stake in Comfort Systems USA (NYSE:FIX) by 33.7%, selling 9,681 shares to now own 19,034 shares valued at $15.7 million. Despite this sale, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of $1,011.75, with some targets recently increasing to $1,140-$1,200. The company, with a market cap of $36.5 billion, recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.60 per share.

feed.title: Intel Stock Climbs After Trump Praises Company Post Meeting With CEO
feed.summary: Intel's stock rose nearly 2% in after-market trading following former President Donald Trump's positive remarks on the company's new Core Ultra Series 3 processors, which are designed, built, and packaged in the U.S. Trump highlighted the government's 10% stake in Intel, making it the largest shareholder, and praised the company's manufacturing efforts in the U.S. Amid market share losses to rivals, Intel replaced its CEO in March 2025, and retail sentiment regarding INTC shares remains 'extremely bullish' on Stocktwits.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor: The $1.65 Trillion Gatekeeper of the AI Boom
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is experiencing significant growth, nearing its all-time high valuation at $1.65 trillion, driven by increasing demand for AI infrastructure. The company's unique pure-play foundry model and dominance in advanced chip manufacturing make it indispensable for major tech firms, effectively acting as a "toll road" for the digital economy. TSMC is also mitigating geopolitical risks through global diversification and successful execution of advanced technologies like 2nm chips and CoWoS packaging.

feed.title: Why is AMD stock plunging sharply on Thursday?
feed.summary: AMD stock experienced a significant drop on Thursday, falling nearly 3% to $204.53, despite its substantial 77% rally in 2025 and new product announcements at CES. The plunge is attributed to broader sector pressures, intense competition from Intel's recent product launches, and investor profit-taking. Additionally, geopolitical uncertainties and technical factors contributed to the sharp decline, highlighting the volatility of high-beta chip stocks.

feed.title: Belite Bio stock hits all-time high at 169.88 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Belite Bio Inc. ADR (BLTE) stock reached an all-time high of $169.88, reflecting a significant 193.5% increase over the last year, indicating strong investor confidence. Despite this, InvestingPro Tips note the company is not currently profitable, and analysts do not anticipate profitability this year. Recent positive Phase 3 trial results for its drug Tinlarebant have led to raised price targets and an Overweight/Outperform rating from various analysts, alongside a public offering of ADSs.

feed.title: Cadence Advances Chiplet Development
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. announced a partner ecosystem at CES 2026 to offer pre-validated chiplet solutions based on its physical AI chiplet platform, aiming to reduce complexity and accelerate time to market for AI, data center, and HPC applications. Collaborating with partners like Arm and Samsung Foundry, this initiative provides a comprehensive suite of IP and tools for standards-compliant chiplet development. The ecosystem includes pre-integrated partner IP on Samsung Foundry's SF5A process and leverages Arm's advanced Zena Compute Subsystem, alongside Cadence's UCIe IP for industry-standard die-to-die connectivity.

feed.title: SOXL ETF Rises Nearly 3% Following Key Trading Signal
feed.summary: The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares (SOXL) ETF saw a nearly 3% rise after triggering a "Power Inflow" alert, a bullish indicator based on institutional and retail order flow data. The signal, which occurred at 11:27 AM EST at a price of $48.45, indicated a significant shift towards buying activity. This led to an immediate increase in SOXL's stock price, reaching $49.79, demonstrating the potential of real-time order flow analytics for identifying intraday bullish momentum and potential reversals.

feed.title: Cadence Advances Chiplet Development
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems has announced a partner ecosystem at CES 2026 to offer pre-validated chiplet solutions based on their physical AI chiplet platform. This initiative, collaborating with partners like Arm and Samsung Foundry, aims to reduce engineering complexity and accelerate time-to-market for chiplets in AI, data center, and HPC applications. The solutions will support various industry standards and leverage Cadence's IP for efficient design and simulation.

feed.title: Micron Raises Fears of a Crowded Trade – Reality Bites for Mu Stock as Momentum Falters
feed.summary: Micron Technology's stock experienced a significant breakout above $300, driven by real improvements in earnings and supply constraints, but a recent sharp decline and rapid ascent have raised concerns about overvaluation and crowded trading. Despite tangible progress and strong market positioning, the company faces high expectations and risks of oversupply in the memory market. While the rally is grounded in real factors unlike speculative AI stocks, its rapid pace suggests that future returns might have been pulled into the present, requiring flawless execution to sustain current levels.

feed.title: GOOG stock rises after hours as Alphabet sets Feb. 4 earnings date; Wiz deal deadline in focus
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) rose 1.1% in after-hours trading as the company announced its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings call for February 4th. Investors are closely watching Google's advertising growth, AI investments, and the EU's antitrust decision on Alphabet's proposed $32 billion acquisition of cybersecurity firm Wiz by February 10th. This period is expected to be volatile for the stock due to regulatory decisions and upcoming earnings.

feed.title: Intel stock rises after Trump touts 'very successful' CEO, applauds government's investment
feed.summary: Intel shares rose after President Donald Trump praised the chipmaker's CEO, Lip-Bu Tan, and highlighted the government's investment in the company. Trump stated the U.S. government, as Intel's biggest shareholder, has gained "Tens of Billions of Dollars" from its August 2025 investment. This comes despite Trump previously calling for Tan's resignation due to concerns over his ties to China.

feed.title: Assessing Amkor Technology (AMKR) Valuation After Needham’s Upgraded AI Packaging Outlook
feed.summary: Needham & Company has upgraded its outlook on Amkor Technology (AMKR) due to its strong position in AI chip packaging, leading to significant share price growth. However, despite the positive sentiment, Amkor's current valuation, with a P/E of 42.3x and a discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicating a fair value significantly below its market price, suggests it may be overvalued. Investors are cautioned to consider whether the AI future is already priced into the stock and to assess the potential risks if market expectations cool.

feed.title: A Look At Analog Devices (ADI) Valuation After AI Fueled Semiconductor Rally And Fed Optimism
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) has seen its share price rise significantly (32.77% in 90 days, 37.95% in one year) due to excitement around AI and potential Federal Reserve policy. Despite this rally, ADI trades at lower P/E and P/B ratios compared to many of its semiconductor peers. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests ADI is currently overvalued at US$299.16 against a narrative fair value of US$282.03, based on optimistic earnings power assumptions.

feed.title: Arteris: Janac K Charles sells $188k in shares
feed.summary: Janac K Charles, President and CEO of Arteris, Inc., sold 11,145 shares of common stock for $188,386 on January 6, 2026, under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. Following the sale, he directly owns 139,487 shares, with additional indirect ownership through other entities. This news comes as Arteris recently reported strong Q3 2025 earnings, an increased price target from Rosenblatt Securities, and announced the acquisition of Cycuity, Inc. to enhance its cybersecurity offerings.

feed.title: Arteris COO Moll sells $23,046 in shares
feed.summary: Arteris, Inc. COO Moll Laurent R. sold 1,420 shares of common stock for $23,046 on January 6, 2026, as part of a pre-arranged trading plan. This transaction leaves Moll with 250,573 shares. The company recently reported strong Q3 2025 earnings, an 18% revenue increase, and the acquisition of Cycuity, Inc. to enhance its product portfolio, leading Rosenblatt Securities to raise its price target to $20.

feed.title: Cemex stock hits 52-week high at $12.04
feed.summary: Cemex stock reached a 52-week high of $12.04, reflecting a significant 111.34% appreciation over the past year. This surge is supported by strong investor confidence, favorable market conditions, and positive analyst upgrades from RBC Capital and BofA Securities, with analysts suggesting further potential up to $14. The company has a market capitalization of $17.34 billion and a "GOOD" financial health rating, and recently announced a cash dividend installment.

feed.title: Nvidia’s Go-To Chipmaker TSMC Sees Revenue Top Estimates
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a key chipmaker for Nvidia, reported December-quarter revenue that exceeded estimates, reaching NT$1.05 trillion ($33.1 billion). This 20% increase over the previous year reinforces optimism for continued global AI spending in 2026, despite some industry concerns about a potential bubble.

feed.title: Nvidia Is Bank of America's Top Chip Pick: AI Demand, Pricing Power Drive Growth
feed.summary: Bank of America Securities analyst Vivek Arya has named Nvidia as his top pick in the semiconductor sector, citing strong demand for AI, solid pricing power, and an attractive valuation. Arya maintains an above-consensus forecast for 30% year-over-year growth in chip sales, driven by secular AI growth and cyclical industrial inventory replenishment. The analyst also highlighted Credo Technology and Analog Devices as strong performers, noting Credo's leadership in active electrical cables for AI clusters and Analog Devices' pricing power due to differentiated products and favorable market mix.

feed.title: Needham Hikes Amkor Technology (AMKR) PT to $50, Cites Explosive CoWoS Demand, AI Growth
feed.summary: Needham has increased its price target for Amkor Technology (AMKR) to $50, maintaining a Buy rating, fueled by an upward revision in AI wafer demand forecasts for 2026. The firm anticipates significant growth in GPU and custom AI chip markets, viewing Amkor as a key beneficiary and leader in the CoWoS advanced packaging sector. Amkor is aggressively expanding its infrastructure with a $7 billion investment in a new Arizona campus and optimizing manufacturing in Japan to capitalize on this demand.

feed.title: AMD Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | ADVANCED MICRO DEVICES (NASDAQ:AMD)
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock, including its current price, key statistics, and historical performance. It also details AMD's business segments, financial highlights, and analyst forecasts, projecting a significant price increase for the next year.

feed.title: Intel Corporation (INTC) Launches Next Generation of AI PCs at CES 2026
feed.summary: Intel Corporation (INTC) launched its next generation of AI PCs at CES 2026 with its Core Ultra Series 3 processors, built on the Intel 18A semiconductor process. This new series is expected to be the most broadly adopted AI PC platform, with pre-orders for the first laptop beginning on January 6, 2026. Following the launch, Melius Research upgraded Intel's stock to Buy, citing confidence in its foundry business and potential adoption of upcoming nodes by companies like Nvidia and Apple.

feed.title: The Intelligent Edge: A Comprehensive Deep Dive into Analog Devices (ADI) in 2026
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) has transformed into a diversified leader in analog technology, crucial for "Intelligent Edge" computing and AI infrastructure, driven by strategic acquisitions and innovation. The company's business model focuses on high barriers to entry and long product lifecycles across industrial, automotive, communications, and consumer segments. Despite potential market cyclicality and geopolitical risks with China, ADI shows strong financial performance and is a key player in electrification and AI integration.

feed.title: Penguin Solutions: The Deep-Value AI Play You've Been Looking For
feed.summary: Penguin Solutions (NASDAQ: PENG) is presented as a deep-value AI stock, trading at low multiples relative to long-term forecasts and outperforming expectations in fiscal Q1 2026. The company specializes in end-to-end AI infrastructure solutions, with catalysts including global AI expansion, product rollouts with NVIDIA, AMD, and Dell, and improved margins. Institutional buying and bullish analyst ratings suggest a robust rebound and significant upside potential for the stock.

feed.title: AMD in 2026: The Systems-Led Challenger in the AI Super-Cycle
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has transformed into a leading challenger in the AI semiconductor market, largely due to its strategic shift towards high-performance computing and data center AI under CEO Lisa Su. With the release of its MI350 and MI400 series accelerators, and significant improvements in its ROCm software, AMD is fiercely competing with Nvidia, despite notable risks like supply chain concentration and market cyclicality. The company's strong financial performance, innovative products like the 2nm MI455X, and growing analyst confidence position it as a foundational pillar in the global AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Teradyne: "I Would Not Step Away From It"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed a strong positive sentiment towards Teradyne (NASDAQ: TER), calling it "one hell of a company" despite its current high valuation. He indicated he would still be a buyer if the stock pulled back, reiterating his long-standing view of Teradyne as a well-run company with strong performance in the semiconductor industry. The article also mentions Teradyne's significant Q3 2025 performance, with its stock up 53% for the quarter.

feed.title: F1: Honda está 'um passo atrás' no desenvolvimento do motor de 2026, afirma portal inglês
feed.summary: A Honda, parceira da Aston Martin para os motores de Fórmula 1 de 2026, estaria atrasada no desenvolvimento de suas unidades de potência para o novo regulamento técnico, segundo o portal britânico The Race. A mudança de foco da Honda após a saída da Red Bull e a realocação de recursos, além da dificuldade com tecnologias de bateria e parcerias com fornecedores de combustível menos experientes, são apontados como fatores para o atraso. Este cenário pode colocar a marca japonesa em desvantagem em relação a concorrentes como Mercedes e Red Bull Powertrains.

feed.title: Aehr Test Systems (NASDAQ:AEHR) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems (NASDAQ: AEHR) reported Q2 2026 earnings, beating expectations with an EPS of -$0.04 against an expected -$0.08. The company is experiencing increased demand for its semiconductor test and burn-in solutions, particularly driven by AI and data center infrastructure growth, leading to reinstated financial guidance for the second half of fiscal 2026 with projected revenues between $25 million and $30 million. They anticipate strong bookings of $60 million to $80 million, primarily from AI accelerator processors, setting the stage for significant growth in fiscal 2027.

feed.title: Kalvista stock surges as Stifel reiterates Buy on strong Ekterly sales
feed.summary: Stifel reiterated a Buy rating and a $39.00 price target for Kalvista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) after the company reported Q4 2025 global Ekterly sales of $35 million, significantly surpassing Street expectations. This strong performance, especially in refills, suggests potential upward revisions to 2026 consensus estimates. Other analysts like Needham and Citizens also raised their price targets, acknowledging Ekterly's successful launch and strong adoption as the first and only oral on-demand treatment for hereditary angioedema.

feed.title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights NVIDIA and Amtech Systems
feed.summary: Zacks Equity Research highlights NVIDIA (NVDA) and Amtech Systems (ASYS) within the Semiconductor – General industry, noting that both companies are well-positioned to benefit from the ongoing AI technological revolution. While geopolitical instability and high valuations present some near-term challenges, the long-term outlook for the semiconductor industry remains bright due to increasing demand across various sectors like AI, HPC, and automotive. Global semiconductor sales are projected for significant growth in the coming years, with NVIDIA driven by its accelerated computing platforms for AI and Amtech Systems poised for stronger results through its capital equipment for semiconductor device packaging.

feed.title: Assessing Amkor Technology (AMKR) Valuation After Needham’s Upgraded AI Packaging Outlook
feed.summary: Needham & Company has upgraded its outlook for Amkor Technology (AMKR) due to its role in AI chip packaging, leading to a significant increase in its stock price. Despite this optimism, the article questions if the AI future is already priced into the stock, noting its current valuation of $52.72 significantly exceeds the analyst target of $39.38 and an intrinsic value estimate of $4.19. The analysis suggests AMKR is overvalued based on P/E ratios and a discounted cash flow model, cautioning investors about potential downside if AI enthusiasm wanes.

feed.title: How CES has ‘reinvented’ itself for the marketing industry
feed.summary: CES is evolving beyond a pure tech show, attracting legacy brands like John Deere and Lego that are using the event to showcase their technological innovations and reach the marketing industry. While AI remains a dominant theme, these traditional companies are highlighting the practical applications of technology, emphasizing usefulness and purpose. The event has also seen an increasing presence of marketing and communications professionals, with many executives leveraging "C Space" to connect with clients and forge deals.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts See a 25.83% Upside in Adeia (ADEA): Can the Stock Really Move This High?
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts project a potential upside of 25.8% for Adeia (ADEA), with a mean price target of $22.75, despite the stock having already gained 33.7% recently. While price targets alone can be misleading, strong analyst agreement and positive earnings estimate revisions, combined with Adeia's Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), suggest a legitimate reason to expect further upside. Investors are cautioned to treat price targets with skepticism but to consider the strong correlation between earnings estimate revisions and stock movements.

feed.title: HP Menampilkan Masa Depan Dunia Kerja di CES 2026
feed.summary: HP showcased its vision for the future of work at CES 2026, focusing on human-centered innovation driven by AI. New product launches included AI PCs like the HP EliteBoard G1a and EliteBook X G2 Series, as well as AI-integrated printers with Microsoft Copilot. HP also announced the unification of its gaming brands under HyperX and the launch of the Future of Work Accelerator 2026 program to support technology access and skills development.

feed.title: Defending at Scale: The Importance of People in Data Center Security
feed.summary: As data center construction accelerates due to AI and cloud demand, robust physical security is paramount. The article emphasizes that despite advanced technology, human error and culture are often the weakest links in security, leading to breaches like the 2025 ATM network attack. Effective data center security relies on a combination of strong people, clear processes, and technology, with consistent training and validations being crucial for operational resilience.

feed.title: HP Reimagines the Desk for the Future of Work
feed.summary: HP has unveiled the HP EliteBoard G1a Next Gen AI PC, a keyboard-integrated AI PC, and the HP Series 7 Pro 4K Monitor, aiming to redefine the modern workspace. These innovations address the growing need for flexible, personalized, and high-performance setups that support AI-driven workflows and seamless collaboration. The new products emphasize mobility, advanced AI capabilities, and robust security features to enhance productivity in evolving work environments.

feed.title: Amkor Technology (NASDAQ:AMKR) Rating Lowered to Hold at Wall Street Zen
feed.summary: Wall Street Zen has downgraded Amkor Technology (NASDAQ:AMKR) from "buy" to "hold," aligning with the stock's average analyst rating, which is now a "Hold" with a consensus price target of $35.00. The company reported strong Q3 earnings, beating EPS and revenue estimates, and provided Q4 guidance. However, insider selling has been observed recently, though insiders still maintain significant ownership.

feed.title: SuRo Capital Corp. Expands AI Infrastructure Investments with $20 Million Commitment to TensorWave
feed.summary: SuRo Capital Corp. announced a $20 million commitment to TensorWave, Inc., expanding its focus on AI infrastructure investments after a strong performance year in 2025, driven by its AI holdings. The company generated significant liquidity, including $0.50 per share in distributions, and aims to continue its disciplined investment approach. SuRo Capital expects to release its detailed financial results for 2025 around March 9, 2026.

feed.title: Rocket Lab: Innovative Company, But Very Expensive Stock (NASDAQ:RKLB)
feed.summary: Rocket Lab's stock surged 188% in 2025 to a $44B market cap, which the author argues is not justified by its current economics, as Electron rocket launches generate insufficient gross profit. The company's valuation heavily depends on the future success and scalability of its Neutron rocket, and any execution failures or delays could lead to substantial downside risk. The author emphasizes that the current premium reflects expectations for flawless execution and strong demand in its Space Systems, alongside government support.

feed.title: NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA Shares Sold by Albion Financial Group UT
feed.summary: Albion Financial Group UT reduced its stake in NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) by 1.6% in the third quarter of 2025, selling 4,847 shares, but it remains their 5th-largest holding. Despite significant insider selling totaling $349.6 million, NVIDIA reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates, and maintains a bullish outlook supported by new product momentum at CES and potential re-entry into the China market. Analysts have a consensus "Buy" rating with an average price target of $262.84, reflective of the company's strong operating performance and growth prospects, tempered by some China-related execution risks.

feed.title: The largest modular quantum computer yet is delayed for extra testing
feed.summary: Rigetti Computing has announced a delay in the general availability of its 108-qubit quantum computing system, Cepheus-1-108Q, moving the target to the end of Q1 2026 for further testing and optimization. The company is making strong progress on fidelity targets, currently achieving 99% median two-qubit gate fidelity, but encountered complexities with tunable couplers requiring another chip iteration. This system, built from twelve 9-qubit chiplets, is set to be Rigetti's largest and the industry's most extensive modular quantum computing system.

feed.title: Park Avenue Securities LLC Makes New $2.99 Million Investment in Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX
feed.summary: Park Avenue Securities LLC initiated a new position in Comfort Systems USA (NYSE: FIX) during Q3, investing approximately $2.99 million in 3,619 shares. The construction company has received largely positive research coverage, with an average price target of $1,011.75 from analysts. Despite net selling by insiders, Comfort Systems USA has increased its quarterly dividend to $0.60 per share, yielding about 0.2%.

feed.title: President Trump, Sec. Lutnick praise Intel after the launch of Panther Lake chips — says investment in company already bringing ‘tens of billions of dollars for the American people’
feed.summary: President Trump and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick praised Intel and its CEO, Lip-Bu Tan, following the successful launch of Panther Lake processors, highlighting the government's 10% stake in the company and its significant financial return. They emphasized Intel's role in revitalizing U.S. chip manufacturing, crediting a White House investment and a major deal with Nvidia for turning around Intel's prior struggles and boosting its stock price. The article details Intel's recent successes, including the new Core Ultra Series 3 chips using its 18A node, and mentions the company's long-term goal of breaking even in its foundry business by 2027 with the 14A node.

feed.title: Lam Research Stock Jumps Amid Analysts’ Enhancing Price Targets
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation's stock (LRCX) has surged by 8.88% due to optimistic market sentiment and technological advancements. Several analysts, including Cantor Fitzgerald, Mizuho, Goldman Sachs, and Aletheia, have raised their price targets for LRCX, citing robust growth in the wafer fabrication equipment demand and increased memory spending. The company demonstrates strong financial fundamentals, including high profitability margins and efficient capital management, despite a high PE ratio, indicating investor confidence in its future growth trajectory.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Techs (GEHC) Stock Price, Quote, News & History
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of GE HealthCare Techs (GEHC) stock, including its current price, key statistics, recent news, and analyst ratings. It highlights the company's financial performance, market position, and future outlook, while also addressing frequently asked questions about purchasing stock, competitors, dividends, and earnings.

feed.title: Nvidia resets the economics of AI factories, again
feed.summary: Nvidia's recent announcements at CES 2026, particularly the new Vera Rubin platform, have significantly reset the economics of AI factories by introducing extreme co-design across its entire system architecture. The company is driving annual performance improvements of five times and throughput of ten times, shifting the focus from individual chip performance to system-level efficiency, specifically tokens per rack. This comprehensive redesign solidifies Nvidia's dominant position, widening the gap with competitors and forcing ecosystem players to align with this new paradigm where historical benchmarks like Moore's Law are accelerated tenfold.

feed.title: First Solar, Arm, SolarEdge, Kopin, Wolfspeed: Top 5 Semiconductor Stocks With Highest Weekly Jump In Retail Chatter
feed.summary: This article identifies the top five semiconductor stocks that experienced the highest weekly increase in retail investor chatter. The list includes First Solar, Arm, SolarEdge Technologies, Kopin, and Wolfspeed, highlighting the growing investor interest in these companies within the semiconductor sector. The analysis is based on a platform tracking retail sentiment.

feed.title: The Truth About STMicroelectronics N.V.: Why This ‘Invisible’ Chip Giant Is Suddenly Everywhere
feed.summary: This article examines STMicroelectronics N.V., an under-the-radar European chip manufacturer that is a significant player in the semiconductor industry. While not as flashy as Nvidia or AMD, STMicroelectronics is crucial for EVs, embedded AI, and industrial smart home tech. The company is positioned as a long-term investment due to its diversification and role in essential, growing sectors, offering a potentially quieter but valuable alternative to high-hype AI stocks.

feed.title: Assessing Marvell Technology (MRVL) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Marvell Technology (MRVL) has experienced recent share price weakness, with a 10% decline over the past month, leading to renewed interest in its valuation. Despite the pullback, the company reports substantial annual revenue and net income, with a detailed cash flow analysis suggesting it is undervalued at $91.73 compared to its current price of $83.22. However, this positive outlook is contingent on continued demand from hyperscale data centers and successful R&D translation into profitable projects.

feed.title: How Is Advanced Micro Devices' Stock Performance Compared to Other Semiconductor Stocks?

feed.title: AI drives memory boom, but chipmakers refuse to rush: WSJ (MU:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: The artificial intelligence boom is significantly increasing demand for memory chips and storage devices, leading to higher prices and profits for manufacturers. However, despite the surging demand, chipmakers are exercising caution in expanding production capacity due to past boom-and-bust cycles and a lack of long-term supply commitments. This disciplined approach is making investors optimistic that the current tight supply and spending restraint will prolong the industry's upcycle, resulting in rising stock prices for memory and storage companies.

feed.title: 5 AI Stocks That Could Replicate NVIDIA’s Decade of Dominance

feed.title: Former Intel CEO warns US chip comeback still has long way to go
feed.summary: Former Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger states that despite recent Intel chip manufacturing milestones, the U.S. still has a long way to go to reclaim chip production from Asia. He emphasizes that the only metric that matters is the number of wafers built in America, and that bringing manufacturing back to the U.S. will take time as the industry took decades to shift to Asia. This comes as the Trump administration pushes to strengthen U.S. chip manufacturing and has taken a stake in Intel, while Gelsinger noted that other major designers like Nvidia and AMD also need to commit to manufacturing on American soil.

feed.title: Should You Forget Nvidia and Buy These 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Right Now?
feed.summary: While Nvidia is a dominant force in AI infrastructure, increased competition and its immense stock run suggest exploring alternatives. Broadcom stands out in the ASIC market, helping companies design custom AI chips, with significant projected revenue growth. AMD, though a distant second in GPUs, is gaining traction in the AI inference market through key partnerships and forecasts strong data center revenue growth.

feed.title: Intel, Infineon, Synopsys Back European Scaleup Program

The program, headquartered in Dresden, Germany, focuses on connecting and mentoring startups and addressing the "Pre to B" phase challenges, with plans to expand its partner ecosystem and focus on the future of computing.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific And NVIDIA Partner To Advance AI-Powered Laboratory Automation
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific and NVIDIA have announced a strategic collaboration to integrate AI-based solutions and laboratory automation. This partnership aims to modernize manual lab processes by embedding AI into instruments and connecting lab infrastructure with NVIDIA's AI platform. The goal is to enhance automation, accuracy, and speed in laboratories globally, making scientific discovery more efficient and accessible.

feed.title: SEALSQ Announces FY 2025 Preliminary Unaudited Key Operational and Financial Metrics
feed.summary: SEALSQ Corp (NASDAQ: LAES) announced preliminary unaudited key operational and financial metrics for FY 2025, reporting a 66% year-on-year revenue growth to $18 million. The company's strong balance sheet boasts over $425 million in cash, supporting its expansion in the quantum ecosystem and its $200 million pipeline for 2026-2028. SEALSQ is focused on scaling production and R&D for post-quantum security infrastructure, with significant progress in smart meter, PKI solutions, and global adoption of its QS7001 Post-Quantum Secure Chip.

feed.title: Semiconductor sales jump 21% Y/Y backed by AI surge, Gartner says (SMH:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Worldwide semiconductor revenue increased by 21% year-over-year to $793 billion in 2025, according to preliminary results from Gartner. This significant growth was primarily propelled by the robust demand for artificial intelligence, with AI semiconductors contributing nearly one-third of total sales. The surge in AI-related components, including processors, high-bandwidth memory, and networking components, continues to fuel the market.

feed.title: Coherent launches optical filter with industry-leading resolution By Investing.com

feed.title: Aclaris begins phase 1b trial of ATI-052 for atopic dermatitis
feed.summary: Aclaris Therapeutics, Inc. has initiated a Phase 1b proof-of-concept trial for ATI-052 in patients with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis, a randomized, blinded study primarily focusing on safety and tolerability. The company plans a second Phase 1b trial for asthma in Q1 2026, with top-line results from both studies expected in the second half of 2026. ATI-052 is a bispecific antibody designed to inhibit key pathways in allergic inflammation and has received positive analyst ratings following promising Phase 1a interim results.

feed.title: Metagenomi changes name, advances hemophilia A gene therapy program By Investing.com
feed.summary: Metagenomi, Inc. has changed its corporate name to Metagenomi Therapeutics, Inc. and is strategically shifting its focus to later-stage preclinical assets, particularly its hemophilia A gene therapy program, MGX-001. The company reported positive preclinical results for MGX-001, demonstrating "curative factor VIII activity" in non-human primates, and plans to submit IND/CTA applications in Q4 2026. Despite a significant stock price decline over the past year and a negative gross profit margin, InvestingPro analysis suggests the company is undervalued, holds more cash than debt, and expects its cash runway to extend through Q4 2027.

feed.title: Why Advanced Micro Devices Stock Popped Today
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock jumped 5.7% after KeyBanc analyst John Vinh upgraded it to "overweight" with a $270 price target, citing strong demand for its AI chips. Vinh's supply chain checks indicate AMD is almost sold out of server CPUs for 2026 and may consider a price increase. He projects AI revenue for AMD to hit $14 billion or $15 billion this year, potentially making AI one-third of the company's total revenue.

feed.title: SK Hynix Bets $13 Billion To Lock In AI Memory Dominance
feed.summary: SK Hynix is investing 19 trillion won (approximately $13 billion) to build a new advanced semiconductor packaging and testing facility (P&T7) in South Korea to strengthen its lead in the global AI chip supply chain. This investment by the world's top supplier of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) to Nvidia comes amid intensifying competition from rivals like Samsung and Micron due to soaring demand for HBM driven by AI and generative AI workloads. The new plant, expected to be operational by 2028, will focus on advanced packaging, integrating with its next-generation DRAM manufacturing facility, M15X, to create a vertically integrated workflow.

feed.title: Global Semiconductor Revenue Grew 21% in 2025, reports Gartner

feed.title: How Character.ai handles 1B daily chats with half the GPU cost now

This collaboration between DigitalOcean, Character.ai, and AMD optimizes hardware and software for high-volume, latency-sensitive AI workloads, supporting over a billion daily queries. The platform's integrated approach to AI infrastructure emphasizes predictable performance, operational simplicity, and cost efficiency in production.
